target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must close ISS-002 by specifying the fraud detection baseline method concretely. The fix: nightly job after provider loads computes rolling 30-day average gallons per fill-up per vehicle stored in vehicle_fuel_baselines table; fraud_detection_dag flags transactions where actual_gallons exceeds 150% of rolling avg or merchant_state does not match vehicle home_state. Also update data_model to add vehicle_fuel_baselines entity. This is a weak-quality plan -- some sections remain thinner than ideal but ISS-002 is addressed.

fix_report.action_taken: Added vehicle_fuel_baselines Snowflake table updated nightly by compute_baselines_task; fraud_detection_dag flags transactions where gallons > 1.5 * rolling_30d_avg_gallons OR merchant_state != vehicle home_state; baseline computation uses windowed Snowflake SQL over last 30 days of canonical fuel_transactions
fix_report.expected_outcome: Fraud detection logic is fully specified and implementable; vehicle_fuel_baselines table provides auditable baseline for each flag

target_output.title: Fleet Fuel Transaction Pipeline v2
target_output.executive_summary: Nightly AWS MWAA Airflow pipeline consolidating fuel transactions from WEX (SFTP CSV), Comdata (OAuth2 REST API), and FleetCor (SFTP CSV) into Snowflake. Canonical normalization with PAN truncation, rolling-baseline fraud detection, and cost center rollups serve fleet finance analysts. Round 2 adds concrete fraud detection baseline specification using 30-day rolling averages stored in vehicle_fuel_baselines.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three parallel provider ingest DAGs (wex_ingest_dag, comdata_ingest_dag, fleetcor_ingest_dag) run nightly in MWAA; each fetches raw files to S3 staging, normalizes to canonical fuel_transaction schema, truncates card PAN to last 4 digits, and executes Snowflake COPY INTO on raw schema. After all three complete, fraud_detection_dag runs compute_baselines_task then flag_transactions_task. dbt models in transformed schema produce cost_center_rollups and fraud_summary views consumed by Tableau dashboards.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.12, Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA. Snowflake for data warehouse with raw and transformed schemas. dbt Core for Snowflake transformations. S3 for staging and 7-year Glacier IA archive. GitHub Actions for CI. Terraform for MWAA and S3 infrastructure. AWS Secrets Manager for SFTP and API credentials with 90-day rotation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: WEX SFTP Ingestion: SFTP fetch of daily transaction CSV, schema validation, canonical normalization. Comdata OAuth2 Ingestion: client_credentials token fetch, REST API pagination for daily transactions, canonical normalization. FleetCor SFTP Ingestion: SFTP fetch of daily transaction CSV, format normalization. Fraud Detection: nightly rolling-baseline computation and transaction flagging. Cost Center Allocation: vehicle_to_cost_center lookup applied during normalization. Fleet Manager Alerts: SNS email on fraud flag threshold breach.
target_output.system_components: AWS MWAA Airflow cluster (private VPC subnets, us-east-1), S3 staging bucket (24h lifecycle), S3 archive bucket (Glacier IA, 7-year retention), Snowflake data warehouse (raw schema and transformed schema), dbt Core models for Snowflake transformations, AWS Secrets Manager (SFTP creds, Comdata OAuth2 client secret, Snowflake service account), SNS topic for fraud alert emails, NAT gateway for outbound SFTP and API calls.
target_output.workflows: WEX and FleetCor Ingest: SFTPOperator fetches daily CSV to S3 staging; PythonOperator validates CSV column schema; normalize_transaction() maps WEX fields to canonical schema (provider, card_last4, vehicle_id, merchant fields, gallons, price, total, transaction_date); S3ToSnowflakeOperator COPY INTO fuel_transactions_raw; on success, move staging file to archive bucket with Glacier IA storage class. Comdata Ingest: PythonOperator fetches OAuth2 bearer token from Secrets Manager client_credentials; paginates Comdata REST API /transactions endpoint for yesterday; normalizes to canonical schema; stages to S3; COPY INTO. Fraud Detection: compute_baselines_task runs Snowflake SQL computing rolling 30-day avg gallons per vehicle per fill-up and updates vehicle_fuel_baselines table; flag_transactions_task joins fuel_transactions_raw with vehicle_fuel_baselines and vehicle_assignments to flag rows where actual_gallons > baseline_gallons * 1.5 OR merchant_state != home_state, writing to fraud_flags table; SNS notification if fraud flag count exceeds daily threshold.
target_output.data_model: fuel_transactions (id UUID, provider ENUM wex comdata fleetcor, card_last4 CHAR4, vehicle_id, driver_id, merchant_id, merchant_name, merchant_state CHAR2, transaction_date DATE, gallons DECIMAL, price_per_gallon DECIMAL, total_amount DECIMAL, cost_center_id, fraud_flag BOOLEAN, fraud_reason TEXT, loaded_at TIMESTAMP). vehicle_fuel_baselines (vehicle_id PK, rolling_30d_avg_gallons DECIMAL, baseline_updated_at TIMESTAMP, sample_count INT). fuel_cards (card_last4, vehicle_id, carrier_id, status, valid_from, valid_to). merchant_locations (merchant_id, name, address, state, lat, lng). vehicle_assignments (vehicle_id, driver_id, cost_center_id, home_state, effective_from, effective_to). cost_centers (id, name, manager_id, budget_monthly). fraud_flags (id, transaction_id, flag_type ENUM out_of_territory volume_spike after_hours, baseline_value DECIMAL, actual_value DECIMAL, flagged_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: No external API. Internal Airflow DAG interfaces: wex_ingest_dag, comdata_ingest_dag, fleetcor_ingest_dag each accept execution_date parameter for backfill runs. Snowflake transformed schema views: v_cost_center_fuel_spend (cost_center, period, total_gallons, total_spend), v_fraud_summary (vehicle_id, flag_count, flag_types, last_flagged_date) consumed by Tableau. Airflow REST API used internally by CI health-check job to verify DAG last run status.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PCI-DSS scope reduction: card PAN truncated to last 4 digits in normalize_transaction() before any S3 write; raw provider files with full PANs are never written to S3 -- normalization happens in MWAA task memory. S3 staging bucket policy restricts access to MWAA execution IAM role only; 24-hour lifecycle deletes staging files after Snowflake load. Secrets Manager stores SFTP private keys, Comdata OAuth2 client secret, and Snowflake service account password with 90-day automatic rotation. Snowflake raw schema insert-only for pipeline role; transformed schema read-only for analyst role. MWAA runs in private VPC subnets with no public IP; NAT gateway for outbound SFTP/API traffic only.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on PR: pylint and black format check for DAG files, pytest for normalize_transaction and detect_fraud_flags unit tests with fixture CSV samples, Terraform plan. On main merge: Terraform apply for infrastructure changes, DAG files synced to MWAA S3 DAG bucket. Nightly schedule: provider DAGs at 00:00 UTC, fraud_detection_dag at 02:30 UTC (after provider loads expected complete), cost_center_rollup dbt job at 03:30 UTC. CloudWatch alarm if fraud_detection_dag does not reach success state by 04:00 UTC.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch metrics: MWAA task success rate, task duration p95, worker queue depth. Airflow email alerts on DAG failure to data-ops@fleet.com. Daily health-check DAG queries Snowflake INFORMATION_SCHEMA.LOAD_HISTORY to verify row counts match expected ranges per provider. Fraud alert SNS notification to fleet-finance@fleet.com when daily fraud_flags count exceeds 10 or any single transaction exceeds $500 flagged.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Projected $850/month: MWAA $420, Snowflake $280, S3 $80, Secrets Manager and NAT $70. Snowflake virtual warehouse auto-suspends after 5 minutes saving idle compute cost. Glacier IA for 7-year archive at $0.004/GB/month vs S3 Standard at $0.023/GB/month saves $140/month at 1TB archive size. Pipeline parallelism keeps nightly window under 2 hours for current volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 WEX Integration (8 weeks, complete): WEX SFTP ingest DAG, canonical normalization, PAN truncation, Snowflake raw load, basic fraud flag for out-of-territory, Terraform IaC, CI pipeline. Phase 2 Full Pipeline and Fraud Baseline (current round): Comdata OAuth2 adapter, FleetCor SFTP adapter, vehicle_fuel_baselines rolling-average computation, fraud_detection_dag with 150% threshold and territory check, dbt cost_center_rollups model, SNS fraud alerts. Phase 3 Analytics and Alerting: Tableau dashboard integration, predictive cost modeling, real-time Kinesis stream for high-value fraud alerts.
target_output.development_guidelines: DAG files must pass pylint score 9.0 or above and black format check in CI. Unit tests required for normalize_transaction() covering WEX, Comdata, and FleetCor fixture CSVs. Fraud detection logic tested with synthetic vehicle_fuel_baselines and known-fraud fixture transactions. No hardcoded credentials anywhere in DAG code -- all via Airflow connections backed by Secrets Manager. dbt models require documentation and schema.yml tests (not_null, unique, accepted_values) for fuel_transactions key columns.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Comdata API rate limit during large backfill runs causes OAuth2 token expiry mid-job. Mitigation: token refresh logic in COMDataAPIOperator before each paginated request; exponential backoff on 429 responses. Risk: Snowflake virtual warehouse auto-suspend causing cold-start delay inside nightly window. Mitigation: MWAA DAG pre-warms Snowflake warehouse 5 minutes before COPY INTO tasks via SQL ALTER WAREHOUSE RESUME. Tradeoff: COPY INTO pattern over Snowpipe for predictable nightly batch control vs real-time micro-batch; acceptable given finance team's daily reporting cadence.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected COPY INTO over Snowpipe for nightly batch control. Confirmed PAN truncation in MWAA task memory (never written to S3 in full form). Fraud baseline window set to 30 days balancing seasonal variation sensitivity with sufficient sample size. Comdata auth confirmed as OAuth2 client_credentials flow per provider documentation review.

metadata.notes: Fleet fuel transaction pipeline in Snowflake; round 2 closes fraud detection baseline specification; weak quality with some thin sections.

project_goal.value: AI-powered predictive maintenance system migrating commercial fleets from reactive scheduling to ML-driven failure prediction using DTC codes, mileage, and sensor trends
project_goal.rationale: Fleet operators lose $8,000-15,000 per unplanned breakdown; predictive maintenance reduces mobile breakdowns by 40-60%

target_users.value: Fleet managers, maintenance technicians, and drivers at commercial vehicle fleet operators
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: ML prediction pipelines and LLM-assisted maintenance guidance are core capabilities

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Dashboard, prediction API, DTC ingestion, Azure AD auth, LLM repair guidance, analytics, and telematics integrations all required

complexity_level.rationale: Per-component ML models, LLM RAG, and AKS microservices make this architecturally complex

risk_level.rationale: Safety-critical predictions; incorrect failure prediction could delay maintenance on a vehicle with brake failure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Fleet operator vehicle health and maintenance records are commercially sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Fleet manager dashboard requires Azure AD authentication; no public access

access_model.value: Azure AD OIDC with RBAC roles: fleet_manager, maintenance_tech, driver, system_admin
access_model.rationale: Role-based access controls maintenance record visibility and work order creation

feature_scope.value: Per-component failure prediction models, fleet manager dashboard with risk scores, automatic work order generation, LLM-assisted repair guidance, driver push notifications, telematics DTC ingestion, maintenance history import
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for predictive maintenance platform

mvp_scope.value: Brake and engine failure prediction models, fleet manager risk dashboard, work order creation in maintenance system, DTC ingestion from Samsara telematics
mvp_scope.rationale: High-ROI components first; brake and engine failures are most costly breakdowns

security_baseline.value: Azure AD OIDC, RBAC for all endpoints, AKS workload identity, Azure Key Vault for secrets, TLS 1.3, audit log for prediction access and work order creation
security_baseline.rationale: High-risk AI system requires comprehensive auth and audit trail

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for dashboard load performance; TypeScript for type safety

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: Python ecosystem for ML model serving; FastAPI for async API performance

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for operational data, Azure Blob Storage for model artifacts and sensor history, Redis 7 for prediction caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational operational data; Blob for ML artifact and training data storage

hosting_target.value: Azure AKS East US 2
hosting_target.rationale: Enterprise AKS with Azure AD integration; Azure-only constraint from enterprise agreement

privacy_retention_policy.value: Vehicle maintenance records retained 10 years per DOT compliance; sensor telemetry raw data 2 years; prediction audit logs 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Fleet operators subject to DOT record retention requirements

future_scope.value: Multi-fleet SaaS offering, tire and transmission failure models, mobile technician app, parts procurement system integration
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 platform expansion roadmap

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Samsara telematics; Azure only for data residency; model inference must return predictions within 2 seconds p99
constraints.rationale: Existing telematics contract and enterprise Azure agreement

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor plus Grafana for AKS metrics; prediction confidence score distribution tracked; MLflow model drift detection alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: MLOps observability required for production AI system

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD; Argo CD for GitOps AKS deployments; MLflow for model lifecycle management
execution_preference.rationale: Enterprise Azure DevOps toolchain alignment

compliance_context.value: SOC 2 Type II for data handling; ISO 55001 asset management alignment; GDPR for EU fleet operator vehicle and driver location data
compliance_context.rationale: Enterprise fleet operators require SOC 2 attestation for vendor approval

llm_integration.value: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o for LLM-assisted repair guidance grounded in OEM service manuals via Azure AI Search RAG; technician queries a component fault and receives step-by-step diagnostic procedure
llm_integration.rationale: LLM reduces technician lookup time for unfamiliar DTC codes and repair procedures

project.goal: ML-driven fleet predictive maintenance with LLM repair guidance
project.target_users: Fleet managers, maintenance technicians, drivers
project.timeline: MVP brake and engine models in 16 weeks

backend.framework: FastAPI async

security.auth: Azure AD OIDC
security.secrets: Azure Key Vault via AKS workload identity

data.blob: Azure Blob Storage for ONNX artifacts and Parquet sensor history

reasoner_reviews.product: Predictive maintenance scope is well-defined with brake and engine models as MVP. LLM repair guidance adds technician value. Model retraining schedule was resolved in round 2 with weekly Azure ML pipeline. The two remaining issues are contract violations: Redux Toolkit in frontend stack and AWS EKS in deployment section -- both must be fixed in round 3.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI prediction service with ONNX runtime is the right pattern for sub-2-second inference. Per-component MLflow models allow independent deployment and rollback. The plan's fix_report claims to fix both contract violations but the technology_stack section still contains Redux Toolkit reference and deployment section still references AWS EKS -- these violations make this plan intentionally flawed.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD OIDC with RBAC is correct for this high-risk system. Prediction audit logging must capture risk_score and confidence values for SOC 2 accountability. Azure Key Vault via workload identity is the right secrets management pattern for AKS.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure-only constraint is explicit in the contract. Any reference to AWS infrastructure in the plan is a contract violation. The weekly retraining schedule on Azure ML compute is cost-effective with Spot instances.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with server components for fleet dashboard initial render; client components for real-time prediction score polling
frontend.pages_and_flows: Fleet Risk Dashboard (vehicle grid with component risk scores), Vehicle Detail (per-component prediction history, DTC event log, maintenance timeline), Work Order Management, Technician Repair Guidance (LLM streaming interface), Driver Notification Preferences
frontend.state_management: React Query for prediction scores and work order data with 5-minute cache TTL; Zustand for UI state including selected vehicle, active risk filters, and notification preferences
frontend.frontend_risks: Prediction score staleness: React Query 5-minute TTL may show stale risk levels during rapid DTC event burst; mitigated by SSE push for high-confidence critical component predictions

backend.service_design: Microservices on AKS: prediction-api (FastAPI, ONNX Runtime, Redis cache), ingestion-worker (Samsara webhook consumer, DTC normalization to PostgreSQL), daily-scoring-job (AKS CronJob, scores all vehicles), llm-guidance-service (Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with Azure AI Search RAG), work-order-service (CRUD plus maintenance system webhook)
backend.api_patterns: REST via FastAPI: GET /v1/vehicles/{vehicle_id}/predictions for component risk scores, POST /v1/guidance/{vehicle_id}/{component} streams LLM repair guidance via SSE, POST /v1/work-orders creates work order, GET /v1/fleet/risk-summary for dashboard aggregate
backend.llm_integration_design: llm-guidance-service retrieves top-3 OEM manual sections from Azure AI Search vector index by DTC code and component type; constructs system prompt with retrieved OEM context and vehicle maintenance history summary; calls Azure OpenAI GPT-4o streaming API; streams response via SSE to Next.js technician interface; content filtering enabled via Azure OpenAI safety configuration
backend.background_jobs: daily-scoring-job CronJob at 03:00 UTC scores all active vehicles against MLflow production ONNX models and caches results in Redis with 6h TTL; weekly retraining pipeline fetches 90-day DTC and maintenance history, trains per-component gradient boosting models, validates against holdout (precision >0.80), exports to ONNX, registers in MLflow staging for manual promotion

security.auth_design: Azure AD OIDC via NextAuth.js on frontend; FastAPI validates Azure AD JWT on every request using MSAL Python; AKS workload identity for pod-level access to Key Vault, Blob Storage, and PostgreSQL
security.authorization_model: fleet_manager: full read and write on predictions and work orders; maintenance_tech: read predictions, create and update work orders; driver: read own vehicle predictions only; system_admin: full access including model deployment approval
security.audit_and_logging_controls: prediction_audit_log captures vehicle_id, component, risk_score, confidence, requesting_user_id, and accessed_at for every prediction access; work order creation logged; all audit events forwarded to Azure Monitor Log Analytics for SOC 2 evidence
security.security_risks: LLM prompt injection via technician input; mitigated by system prompt separation from user content and Azure OpenAI content filtering; guidance responses return structured step list only, not raw OEM manual text

data.entities: vehicles (id UUID, vin, make, model, year, fleet_id, mileage_km, last_service_date, status), maintenance_records (id, vehicle_id, service_date, component, work_performed, technician_id, mileage_at_service, dtc_codes_cleared text array), dtc_events (id, vehicle_id, dtc_code, component, severity ENUM critical warning info, first_seen, last_seen, occurrence_count, resolved_at), failure_predictions (id, vehicle_id, component ENUM brake engine transmission tire, risk_score decimal 0-1, confidence, model_version, predicted_at, prediction_horizon_days), work_orders (id, vehicle_id, component, priority, assigned_tech_id, status ENUM open in_progress completed, created_at, completed_at, resolution_notes), component_lifecycles (id, vehicle_id, component, installed_at, mileage_at_install, expected_life_miles)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for operational entities; Azure Blob Storage for ONNX model artifacts, training dataset Parquet files, and raw sensor telemetry; Redis 7 caches failure_predictions per vehicle with 6-hour TTL
data.data_risks: Training data skew: historical maintenance records underrepresent failures on newer vehicle models with few mileage observations; mitigated by per-vehicle-cohort models with minimum 50-sample threshold before MLflow production promotion

devops.deployment_topology: Azure AKS East US 2 with namespaces: fleet-prod (prediction-api, ingestion-worker, work-order-service), fleet-ml (llm-guidance-service, daily-scoring-job), fleet-infra (Redis, monitoring sidecars). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in same VNet. Azure Cache for Redis or in-cluster Redis pod.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: PR pipeline runs pytest, mypy, Ruff lint, Docker build; merge to main triggers Argo CD sync to fleet-staging AKS namespace; manual promotion gate to fleet-prod. MLflow model promotion: staging model validated against holdout dataset before registered as production; Argo CD rolling update deploys new ONNX artifact to prediction-api pods.
devops.devops_risks: AKS node pool autoscaling latency during 06:00 AM peak fleet DTC reporting window; mitigated by scheduled pre-scale at 05:30 UTC via AKS scheduled scale action

target_output.title: Fleet Predictive Maintenance AI v3
target_output.executive_summary: ML-driven commercial fleet predictive maintenance platform on Azure AKS East US 2. Per-component ONNX failure prediction models for brake and engine components score active vehicles daily. LLM repair guidance via Azure OpenAI GPT-4o with OEM service manual RAG assists technicians at time of maintenance. Next.js 14 fleet manager dashboard with React Query state management surfaces component risk scores and triggers automated work orders in maintenance system.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 SSR dashboard communicates over HTTPS to FastAPI microservices on Azure AKS East US 2. Ingestion worker consumes Samsara DTC webhook events, normalizes to PostgreSQL 16 dtc_events table. Daily scoring CronJob loads per-component ONNX models from MLflow production registry, runs inference for all active vehicles, caches failure_predictions in Redis 7 with 6h TTL. LLM guidance service retrieves OEM manual context from Azure AI Search and calls Azure OpenAI GPT-4o to generate step-by-step repair procedures. Work order service webhooks into existing maintenance management system when brake or engine risk score exceeds 0.75. Azure AD OIDC secures all endpoints with RBAC roles.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Redux Toolkit for global state management (NOTE: contract violation -- contract confirmed React Query plus Zustand, not Redux Toolkit). Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI async, ONNX Runtime 1.17 for model inference. ML: MLflow 2.x on Azure ML, scikit-learn gradient boosting for training, ONNX export. LLM: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o, Azure AI Search with vector index for OEM manual RAG. Data: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Flexible Server, Redis 7, Azure Blob Storage for model artifacts. Infrastructure: Azure AKS East US 2, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Argo CD.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Fleet Risk Dashboard: vehicle grid with per-component risk scores color-coded by severity refreshed every 5 minutes. Vehicle Detail: component prediction history chart, DTC event timeline, maintenance record history, active work orders. LLM Repair Guidance: technician selects component fault, guidance service streams step-by-step diagnostic procedure grounded in OEM manual context via Azure AI Search. Automated Work Orders: prediction-api triggers work-order-service webhook when brake or engine risk_score exceeds 0.75. Driver Notifications: push notification when vehicle flagged high-risk for next-day operation.
target_output.system_components: prediction-api (FastAPI, ONNX Runtime inference, Redis 7 cache with 6h TTL), ingestion-worker (Samsara webhook consumer, DTC normalization to PostgreSQL), daily-scoring-job (AKS CronJob 03:00 UTC, scores all active vehicles), llm-guidance-service (FastAPI, Azure AI Search RAG plus Azure OpenAI GPT-4o streaming), work-order-service (CRUD API plus maintenance system webhook), Next.js 14 dashboard (AKS ingress), PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Flexible Server, Redis 7 Azure Cache for Redis, Azure Blob Storage for ONNX model artifacts.
target_output.workflows: Vehicle Risk Scoring: daily-scoring-job CronJob at 03:00 UTC queries PostgreSQL for active vehicles with new DTC events since previous scoring; loads per-component ONNX model from MLflow production registry artifact in Azure Blob; runs batch ONNX inference for each vehicle-component pair in 50-vehicle batches; writes failure_predictions rows to PostgreSQL; updates Redis cache key vehicles:{id}:predictions with 6h TTL; high-confidence predictions where risk_score >0.75 trigger work-order-service POST /internal/work-orders webhook. Technician LLM Guidance: tech opens Vehicle Detail in dashboard and clicks 'Get Repair Guidance' for flagged component; frontend calls POST /v1/guidance/{vehicle_id}/{component}; llm-guidance-service queries Azure AI Search for top-3 OEM manual sections by DTC code and component; constructs GPT-4o system prompt with OEM context and vehicle maintenance history summary; streams response via SSE; tech sees step-by-step diagnostic procedure in real time. Model Retraining: weekly Azure ML pipeline fetches 90-day DTC and maintenance_records from PostgreSQL; trains per-component scikit-learn GradientBoostingClassifier; evaluates on 20% holdout (precision >0.80 required); exports to ONNX; registers as staging version in MLflow Model Registry; data engineer reviews metrics and manually promotes to production; Argo CD detects new ONNX artifact in Blob and triggers rolling update on prediction-api pods.
target_output.data_model: vehicles (id UUID PK, vin CHAR17, make, model, year INT, fleet_id UUID FK, mileage_km BIGINT, last_service_date DATE, status ENUM active inactive decommissioned). maintenance_records (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, service_date DATE, component ENUM brake engine transmission tire, work_performed TEXT, technician_id UUID FK, mileage_at_service BIGINT, dtc_codes_cleared TEXT[]). dtc_events (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, dtc_code CHAR6, component, severity ENUM critical warning info, first_seen TIMESTAMP, last_seen TIMESTAMP, occurrence_count INT, resolved_at TIMESTAMP NULLABLE). failure_predictions (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, component ENUM brake engine transmission tire, risk_score DECIMAL CHECK 0-1, confidence DECIMAL, model_version TEXT, predicted_at TIMESTAMP, prediction_horizon_days INT). work_orders (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, component, priority ENUM critical high medium, assigned_tech_id UUID FK NULLABLE, status ENUM open in_progress completed, created_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP NULLABLE, resolution_notes TEXT NULLABLE). component_lifecycles (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID FK, component, installed_at DATE, mileage_at_install BIGINT, expected_life_miles INT). prediction_audit_log (id UUID PK, vehicle_id UUID, component, risk_score DECIMAL, confidence DECIMAL, requesting_user_id UUID, accessed_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: REST via FastAPI with Azure AD JWT required on all endpoints. GET /v1/vehicles/{vehicle_id}/predictions returns failure_predictions array for all components with risk_score and confidence. GET /v1/fleet/risk-summary returns vehicles sorted by max component risk_score for dashboard. POST /v1/guidance/{vehicle_id}/{component} streams LLM step-by-step repair guidance via SSE. POST /v1/work-orders creates work order (fleet_manager and system roles only). GET /v1/work-orders?vehicle_id=&status= lists work orders. All prediction GET requests append row to prediction_audit_log.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OIDC: NextAuth.js frontend validates session; MSAL Python FastAPI middleware validates Azure AD JWT and extracts roles claim on every request. RBAC enforcement: fleet_manager full access; maintenance_tech read predictions and manage work orders; driver read own vehicle only; system_admin full including model promotion. Audit: prediction_audit_log records vehicle_id, component, risk_score, confidence, user_id, and accessed_at for SOC 2 Type II evidence. Azure Key Vault for all secrets (PostgreSQL password, Azure OpenAI key, Samsara webhook HMAC secret) via AKS workload identity; no secrets in environment variables or Docker images. LLM safety: Azure OpenAI content filtering; system prompt separated from user input; guidance responses return structured step list only. TLS 1.3 at AKS ingress.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines CI: pytest with 85% coverage gate, mypy strict, Ruff lint, Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, Terraform plan for infrastructure changes. Argo CD GitOps: merge to main syncs to fleet-staging AKS namespace; manual promotion gate to fleet-prod with smoke test suite. Model training jobs run on AWS EKS auto-scaling node groups with GPU instances for ONNX model training (NOTE: contract violation -- Azure-only constraint requires Azure ML compute, not AWS EKS). MLflow Model Registry staging-to-production requires manual data engineer approval. AKS node pools pre-scaled at 05:30 UTC before 06:00 AM peak DTC ingestion window.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor for AKS pod and node metrics. Grafana dashboards: prediction-api latency p99 and throughput, daily scoring job duration and vehicle count, work order creation rate per component, model confidence score distribution per component per version. MLflow tracking: precision, recall, F1 per component model version with holdout evaluation results. Alert rules: prediction-api p99 >2s triggers PagerDuty P1; daily scoring job failure triggers PagerDuty P2; model confidence distribution shift >15% from training baseline triggers Slack #ml-ops for drift investigation.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Projected $4,200/month Azure: AKS node pools $2,800, Azure Database PostgreSQL Flexible Server $400, Azure OpenAI GPT-4o token costs at estimated guidance volume $350, Azure AI Search $250, Azure Cache for Redis $200, Blob Storage $100, miscellaneous $100. Spot instances for weekly Azure ML training job reduce compute cost by 60%. Redis prediction cache reduces prediction-api database reads by 80% during business hours.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP Brake and Engine Models (16 weeks): Samsara DTC webhook ingestion, brake and engine ONNX models with MLflow lifecycle, fleet risk dashboard with Azure AD auth, work order service with maintenance system webhook, prediction audit logging, Azure DevOps CI and Argo CD deployment. Phase 2 Full Model Suite and LLM Guidance (8 weeks post-MVP): transmission and tire failure prediction models, LLM guidance service with Azure AI Search OEM manual RAG, driver push notifications, model retraining pipeline automation with holdout validation. Phase 3 Multi-Fleet SaaS Expansion: multi-tenant PostgreSQL row-level security, fleet operator self-service onboarding, mobile technician app, parts procurement system integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python: mypy strict mode, Ruff lint with E and W rules, pytest with 85% coverage gate for prediction-api and ingestion-worker. TypeScript: strict mode, ESLint with Next.js recommended ruleset. All FastAPI endpoints must have OpenAPI schema with request and response models documented. ML models require holdout evaluation metrics (precision >0.80) before MLflow production promotion; model cards documenting training data and evaluation results required. LLM guidance: responses must be structured step lists only, never raw OEM manual text. Azure Key Vault integration tested in CI against a dedicated test vault instance with test secrets.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Daily scoring job fails silently and stale predictions shown to fleet managers. Mitigation: Azure Monitor alert if failure_predictions table has no new rows inserted within 25 hours; Redis 6h TTL surfaces prediction staleness to frontend after cache expiry. Risk: LLM hallucination for rare DTC codes with no OEM manual coverage in Azure AI Search index. Mitigation: guidance service adds explicit disclaimer when top search result relevance score is below 0.6; technician instructed to escalate to OEM support line. Tradeoff: per-component ONNX models over single multi-output model increases MLflow deployment complexity but enables independent retraining and rollback when one component model degrades.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Azure OpenAI GPT-4o over self-hosted LLM per Azure-only constraint. Weekly retraining schedule chosen over continuous to avoid label leakage from in-progress maintenance records. Brake and engine models selected for MVP based on fleet operator ROI analysis showing highest breakdown cost for these components. ONNX export chosen over TorchServe for runtime independence from training framework.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed round 3 plan: technology_stack still has Redux Toolkit contradicting React Query+Zustand contract; deployment section still references AWS EKS contradicting Azure-only constraint. ISS-002 and ISS-003 unresolved despite fix_report claims.

project_goal.value: Fleet dispatch web application decomposing a monolithic routing engine into microservices for order assignment, route optimization, driver communication, and real-time ETA tracking for a last-mile delivery operator
project_goal.rationale: Monolithic routing engine cannot scale independently; dispatch and routing have different scaling and deployment lifecycles

target_users.value: Dispatchers managing order assignment and routing, fleet managers overseeing driver performance, and integration partners consuming ETA webhooks
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery workshops

project_class.rationale: Browser-based dispatch console with real-time map interface and admin panel

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, analytics, realtime, admin_panel
capabilities.rationale: Dispatch UI, microservice APIs, PostgreSQL data, Auth0 auth, Google Maps integration, delivery analytics, real-time SSE for ETA updates, and admin configuration panel are all required

complexity_level.rationale: Microservices decomposition with real-time route optimization and SSE event streaming is architecturally complex

risk_level.rationale: Personal data for drivers and delivery recipients; dispatch errors have operational but not safety-critical impact

data_sensitivity.rationale: Driver location data and recipient delivery addresses constitute personal data under GDPR

external_exposure.rationale: Dispatch console requires Auth0 login; ETA webhook consumer APIs use HMAC-signed payloads to integration partners

access_model.value: Auth0 OIDC with RBAC: dispatcher, fleet_manager, admin, integration_partner roles
access_model.rationale: Different roles need different levels of access to route data, driver PII, and system configuration

feature_scope.value: Order assignment and clustering, route optimization with Google Maps Directions API, dispatch console with live driver map, real-time ETA updates via SSE, driver mobile communication, delivery analytics dashboard, service zone management, admin configuration panel
feature_scope.rationale: Full decomposed feature set replacing monolithic routing engine

mvp_scope.value: Order assignment microservice, route optimization microservice using Google Maps, dispatch console with live map, basic ETA SSE stream to dispatchers, Auth0 authentication
mvp_scope.rationale: Core dispatch and routing functionality before analytics and partner integrations

security_baseline.value: Auth0 OIDC, JWT validation at API gateway, HTTPS everywhere, GDPR-compliant PII handling with driver location data pseudonymization in analytics, HMAC-signed ETA webhooks for integration partners
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data handling and partner webhook integrity require explicit security controls

frontend_stack.value: Vue 3 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Existing frontend team has Vue 3 expertise; Composition API for complex real-time dispatch state management

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Fastify microservices
backend_stack.rationale: Fastify for high-throughput request handling; Node ecosystem for Google Maps SDK and Auth0 integration

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS extension for geospatial queries, Redis 7 for route cache and SSE connection registry
data_platform.rationale: PostGIS enables service zone polygon queries and geospatial stop clustering; Redis for real-time state

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with per-service independent scaling; existing AWS infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Driver precise location data retained 30 days then pseudonymized in analytics; delivery recipient address retained 90 days then deleted; delivery event history retained 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization and retention requirements for driver and recipient personal data

future_scope.value: Recipient-facing ETA tracking page, predictive delivery time ML model, multi-depot optimization, third-party carrier integration
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 product roadmap items

constraints.value: Route optimization API calls must complete within 3 seconds for routes up to 25 stops; Google Maps API budget under $2,000/month; must replace monolithic routing engine with zero downtime migration
constraints.rationale: Dispatcher SLA and cost constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for all Fargate services, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty alerts on route optimization timeout or SSE connection drops
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; GitHub Actions CI/CD; trunk-based development with feature flags for microservice migration
execution_preference.rationale: Team workflow and zero-downtime migration requirement

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 6 lawful basis for driver location processing; data subject access rights for drivers; local labor regulations for driver working hours (route assignments must not exceed legal driving limits)
compliance_context.rationale: Driver personal data processing and labor law compliance are required

project.goal: Decompose monolithic routing engine into microservices for order assignment, route optimization, driver communication, and ETA tracking
project.target_users: Dispatchers, fleet managers, integration partners
project.timeline: MVP dispatch and routing in 12 weeks; full platform in 24 weeks

frontend.maps: Google Maps JS SDK for dispatch console map
frontend.state: Pinia for global state, VueQuery for server state
frontend.realtime: SSE for ETA updates and driver position streaming

backend.framework: Fastify per microservice
backend.api: REST APIs per service behind API gateway

security.auth: Auth0 OIDC via API gateway JWT validation
security.webhooks: HMAC-SHA256 signed ETA webhooks for integration partners

data.cache: Redis 7 for route cache and SSE registry
data.partition: Routes and stops partitioned by depot_id

devops.environments: dev, staging, prod

reasoner_reviews.product: The microservices decomposition scope is well-justified by independent scaling requirements: route-optimizer-svc is CPU-intensive and needs separate autoscaling from the lightweight order-assignment-svc. The zero-downtime migration constraint via feature flags is realistic. The 25-stop route optimization latency SLA of 3 seconds is achievable with Google Maps Directions API but will require caching of common route segments.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify microservices on Fargate is the right pattern for this decomposition. PostGIS on PostgreSQL is essential for service zone polygon queries and stop clustering. Redis sorted sets are appropriate for the SSE connection registry tracking active dispatcher connections. The migration strategy should use a strangler-fig pattern with LaunchDarkly flags routing traffic from monolith to microservices per capability as each service is validated.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 OIDC at the API gateway is correct. GDPR compliance for driver location data requires explicit data minimization -- precise GPS coordinates should not be logged beyond the operational retention period. HMAC-SHA256 signed webhooks for integration partners prevent ETA data spoofing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Google Maps Directions API cost at 3 seconds per optimization call: with 500 routes optimized daily at $5 per 1000 requests (Directions Advanced), monthly cost is approximately $750, well within the $2,000 budget. Route segment caching in Redis can reduce API calls by 40% for repeated corridor stops.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Composition API; feature modules: dispatch-console, route-management, driver-panel, analytics, admin. Google Maps JS SDK embedded in dispatch-console map component. SSE client managed by useEtaStream composable.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dispatch Console (full-screen map with order queue, driver pins, click-to-assign interface), Route Detail (stop sequence map, ETA timeline, driver communication), Fleet Overview (driver status board, daily completion metrics), Analytics Dashboard (delivery performance, on-time rate, route efficiency), Service Zone Management, Admin Configuration Panel
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores: ordersStore (pending and assigned orders), driversStore (active driver positions, status), routesStore (active routes and stop ETAs). VueQuery for API data fetching with 2-minute stale time for analytics endpoints.
frontend.frontend_risks: Google Maps JS SDK bundle size adds 200KB to initial load; mitigated by dynamic import with loading state; map only loaded on dispatch-console route

backend.service_design: Five Fastify microservices: order-assignment-svc (receives inbound orders, clusters stops by PostGIS proximity and driver availability, emits assignment events), route-optimizer-svc (calls Google Maps Directions API for ordered stop sequences, caches common segments in Redis), driver-comm-svc (SSE connections for driver mobile, push notifications), eta-tracker-svc (ingests driver position updates, recomputes remaining stop ETAs, publishes updates to SSE stream), analytics-svc (aggregates delivery events into daily performance metrics)
backend.api_patterns: REST API per service behind AWS ALB with JWT validation at gateway layer. order-assignment-svc: POST /v1/orders (create order), POST /v1/assignments (assign order to driver), GET /v1/assignments?depot_id=&date=. route-optimizer-svc: POST /v1/routes/optimize (optimize stop sequence), GET /v1/routes/{route_id}. eta-tracker-svc: GET /v1/etas/stream (SSE for dispatcher), POST /v1/positions (driver location update).
backend.failure_handling: route-optimizer-svc: circuit breaker on Google Maps API calls (fallback to cached last-known route segments); Redis cache TTL 4h for optimized routes. eta-tracker-svc: Redis Pub/Sub fanout to multiple SSE connections per dispatcher; reconnection token for SSE resume without re-auth.
backend.scaling_notes: route-optimizer-svc scales independently on CPU (target 60% ECS autoscaling); order-assignment-svc scales on request count; eta-tracker-svc scales on SSE connection count metric from Redis registry

security.auth_design: Auth0 OIDC: Vue 3 frontend uses Auth0 Vue SDK; all API requests carry Auth0 JWT; ALB JWT authorizer validates token before routing to Fargate services. Integration partner webhooks signed with HMAC-SHA256 using per-partner secret stored in AWS Secrets Manager.
security.authorization_model: dispatcher: create orders, view routes, access SSE ETA stream; fleet_manager: full dispatch access plus driver performance analytics; admin: service zone management and system configuration; integration_partner: ETA webhook subscription only, no console access
security.privacy_controls: Driver GPS coordinates pseudonymized in analytics events (grid-level rounding to 0.01 degree); delivery recipient full address stored in orders table with 90-day lifecycle; driver precise location logs deleted after 30 days via nightly PostgreSQL partition drop; GDPR data subject access export endpoint for drivers
security.security_risks: SSE connection leaking driver position to wrong dispatcher tenant; mitigated by depot_id scoping on all SSE subscriptions and JWT claims validation per connection

data.entities: delivery_orders (id, depot_id, recipient_name, delivery_address, address_lat, address_lng, time_window_start, time_window_end, status ENUM pending assigned in_transit delivered failed, created_at). drivers (id, name, email, phone, depot_id, vehicle_id, status ENUM available on_route offline, current_lat, current_lng, updated_at). routes (id, depot_id, driver_id, optimization_date DATE, status ENUM draft active completed, optimized_at, google_maps_route_id). stops (id, route_id, order_id, sequence_num, planned_eta, actual_arrival, status ENUM pending arrived completed skipped). eta_updates (id, route_id, stop_id, previous_eta, new_eta, triggered_by ENUM position_update traffic_event manual, updated_at). service_zones (id, depot_id, name, polygon GEOMETRY POLYGON, active). vehicles (id, driver_id, plate, capacity_kg, status).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS: routes and stops partitioned by depot_id; spatial index on service_zones.polygon for order-to-zone lookup; spatial index on drivers.(current_lat, current_lng) via PostGIS. Redis 7: hash for active driver positions (driver:{id}:position), sorted set for SSE connection registry (etas:stream:{depot_id}), string cache for optimized route JSON (route:{route_id}:optimized).
data.retention_and_deletion: delivery_orders.recipient address NULLed after 90 days via nightly DELETE batch; drivers location logs truncated after 30 days via partition drop; eta_updates retained 2 years for analytics

devops.deployment_topology: Five Fargate services behind single ALB with path-based routing: /api/orders/* -> order-assignment-svc, /api/routes/* -> route-optimizer-svc, /api/positions/* and /api/etas/* -> eta-tracker-svc, /api/drivers/* -> driver-comm-svc, /api/analytics/* -> analytics-svc. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS in us-east-1 (multi-AZ). ElastiCache Redis 7. CloudFront for Vue 3 SPA static assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: PR runs ESLint, Vitest for Vue, Jest for Fastify services, Docker build per service. Merge to main deploys to staging via ECS rolling update. LaunchDarkly feature flags control monolith-to-microservice traffic cutover per capability: FLAG_USE_ROUTE_OPTIMIZER gates route-optimizer-svc rollout.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on each Fargate task. Custom metrics: route_optimization_duration_ms, etas_stream_active_connections, assignment_queue_depth, google_maps_api_calls_per_day. PagerDuty alert on route_optimization_duration_ms p99 >3000ms or etas_stream_reconnect_rate >20/min.
devops.devops_risks: Zero-downtime migration from monolith requires dual-write period where both monolith and order-assignment-svc write to PostgreSQL; mitigated by LaunchDarkly flag with canary rollout at 5% then 25% then 100% traffic

target_output.thinking_summary: Decomposing a monolithic routing engine into five Fastify microservices on Fargate. Key decisions: PostGIS on PostgreSQL for service zone polygon queries and stop geospatial clustering; Redis Pub/Sub for SSE fanout to dispatcher connections; Google Maps Directions API with Redis route segment caching to stay within $2k/month budget; LaunchDarkly strangler-fig migration for zero-downtime cutover. Compliance_context is required because data_sensitivity is personal. No LLM integration needed.
target_output.title: Last-Mile Fleet Dispatch Microservices Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A Vue 3 dispatch web application decomposing a monolithic routing engine into five Fastify microservices on AWS ECS Fargate: order assignment with PostGIS stop clustering, Google Maps route optimization with Redis caching, real-time ETA SSE streaming to dispatchers, driver communication, and delivery analytics. Auth0 OIDC secures the dispatch console with RBAC for dispatchers, fleet managers, and integration partners. LaunchDarkly feature flags enable zero-downtime strangler-fig migration from the monolith.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 TypeScript SPA served via CloudFront communicates over HTTPS to five Fargate microservices behind a single ALB with path-based routing. order-assignment-svc clusters inbound delivery_orders by PostGIS proximity to available drivers and emits assignment events. route-optimizer-svc calls Google Maps Directions API to sequence stops, caches optimized route JSON in Redis for 4 hours. eta-tracker-svc ingests driver GPS position updates via POST /v1/positions, recomputes remaining stop ETAs, and fans out updates to active dispatcher SSE connections via Redis Pub/Sub. driver-comm-svc manages driver SSE connections for route instructions and push notifications. analytics-svc aggregates daily delivery performance metrics. Auth0 JWT validated at ALB before routing to any service. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS stores geospatial entities; ElastiCache Redis 7 manages real-time driver positions and SSE connection registry.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3 TypeScript, Composition API, Pinia (global state), VueQuery (server state), Google Maps JS SDK, Vue Router 4, Vitest. Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4.x per microservice, Fastify JWT plugin, PostGIS-js client for geospatial queries. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS extension (geospatial indexes on service_zones and driver positions). Cache and realtime: ElastiCache Redis 7 (driver positions as hash, SSE registry as sorted set, route cache as string). Infrastructure: AWS ECS Fargate, ALB with path-based routing, CloudFront, Secrets Manager, ACM. Monitoring: Datadog APM. CI: GitHub Actions. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Order Assignment: dispatcher creates delivery_orders; order-assignment-svc queries PostGIS to find stops within driver service_zone polygons; clusters stops by proximity and assigns to nearest available driver. Route Optimization: route-optimizer-svc receives assigned stop list, calls Google Maps Directions API with waypoints optimization, caches result in Redis, returns ordered stop sequence with ETAs. Dispatch Console Map: Vue 3 Google Maps component shows driver pins with real-time positions, pending order markers, and active route polylines; dispatcher can drag-and-drop stops to resequence. Real-Time ETA Stream: dispatchers connect to GET /v1/etas/stream SSE; eta-tracker-svc fans out ETA update events as drivers complete stops or encounter traffic. Driver Communication: driver-comm-svc sends route instructions to driver mobile via SSE; push notification on new assignment. Delivery Analytics: on-time delivery rate per driver, route efficiency score, depot daily throughput, service zone coverage gaps.
target_output.system_components: order-assignment-svc (Fastify, PostGIS stop clustering, assignment queue), route-optimizer-svc (Fastify, Google Maps Directions API client, Redis route cache), eta-tracker-svc (Fastify, position ingestion, Redis Pub/Sub SSE fanout, ETA recomputation), driver-comm-svc (Fastify, driver SSE connections, push notification integration), analytics-svc (Fastify, daily aggregation queries, Pinia-compatible API responses), Vue 3 SPA (CloudFront, Google Maps JS SDK, Pinia, VueQuery), ALB (path-based routing to Fargate services, Auth0 JWT authorizer), Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with PostGIS (multi-AZ us-east-1), ElastiCache Redis 7 (driver positions, SSE connection registry, route cache), Secrets Manager (Auth0 secrets, Google Maps API key, partner HMAC secrets).
target_output.workflows: Order Assignment and Route Optimization: dispatcher receives inbound order in queue; clicks 'Auto-Assign'; order-assignment-svc calls PostGIS ST_Within to find driver whose current position is within the order's service_zone polygon; selects driver with lowest current stop count and earliest availability; creates assignment record; route-optimizer-svc receives assignment event, fetches all pending stops for driver, calls Google Maps Directions API with waypoints=optimize:true for up to 25 stops, stores optimized stop sequence and ETAs as route and stops records, caches JSON in Redis. ETA Recomputation: driver mobile app sends POST /v1/positions every 30 seconds; eta-tracker-svc updates driver:{id}:position Redis hash; computes great-circle distance to next stop; if projected arrival differs from planned_eta by >5 minutes, recomputes all remaining stop ETAs using stored Google Maps route geometry; publishes eta_update event to Redis channel etas:stream:{depot_id}; all active dispatcher SSE connections subscribed to that channel receive the event and update the dispatch console ETA timeline. Stop Completion: driver marks stop completed in mobile app; driver-comm-svc sends POST /v1/stops/{id}/complete to eta-tracker-svc; stop status updated to completed; next stop instructions sent to driver via SSE; on final stop completion, route status set to completed and delivery analytics event emitted.
target_output.data_model: delivery_orders (id UUID PK, depot_id UUID FK, recipient_name TEXT, delivery_address TEXT, address_lat FLOAT, address_lng FLOAT, time_window_start TIME, time_window_end TIME, status ENUM pending assigned in_transit delivered failed, created_at TIMESTAMP). drivers (id UUID PK, name TEXT, email TEXT, depot_id UUID FK, vehicle_id UUID FK, status ENUM available on_route offline, current_lat FLOAT, current_lng FLOAT, updated_at TIMESTAMP). routes (id UUID PK, depot_id UUID FK, driver_id UUID FK, optimization_date DATE, status ENUM draft active completed, optimized_at TIMESTAMP, google_maps_route_id TEXT). stops (id UUID PK, route_id UUID FK, order_id UUID FK, sequence_num INT, planned_eta TIMESTAMP, actual_arrival TIMESTAMP NULLABLE, status ENUM pending arrived completed skipped). eta_updates (id UUID PK, route_id UUID FK, stop_id UUID FK, previous_eta TIMESTAMP, new_eta TIMESTAMP, triggered_by ENUM position_update traffic_event manual, updated_at TIMESTAMP). service_zones (id UUID PK, depot_id UUID FK, name TEXT, polygon GEOMETRY(POLYGON, 4326), active BOOLEAN). vehicles (id UUID PK, driver_id UUID FK, plate TEXT, capacity_kg INT).
target_output.api_design: REST APIs per service behind ALB. order-assignment-svc: POST /v1/orders (create delivery order), POST /v1/assignments (assign order to driver, triggers route optimization), GET /v1/assignments?depot_id=&date= (dispatcher queue). route-optimizer-svc: POST /v1/routes/optimize (optimize stop sequence for driver, returns ordered stops with ETAs), GET /v1/routes/{route_id} (route with stop sequence and ETA timeline). eta-tracker-svc: POST /v1/positions (driver GPS position update), GET /v1/etas/stream (SSE endpoint for dispatcher ETA updates, scoped to Auth0 JWT depot_id claim). analytics-svc: GET /v1/analytics/daily?depot_id=&date= (delivery performance summary). All endpoints require Auth0 JWT. HMAC-SHA256 signed webhooks POST to partner-registered URLs on delivery completion events.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OIDC: Vue 3 frontend uses Auth0 Vue SDK; ALB JWT authorizer validates Auth0 token before routing to Fargate; Fastify JWT plugin re-validates token in service and extracts depot_id and role claims. RBAC enforcement: dispatcher read-write on orders and SSE stream; fleet_manager full dispatch plus analytics; admin service zone and system configuration; integration_partner ETA webhook subscription only. GDPR controls: driver GPS coordinates stored at full precision in operational Redis hash (30-day TTL); pseudonymized to 0.01-degree grid in analytics events; delivery recipient address NULLed from delivery_orders after 90 days via nightly batch job; GDPR data export endpoint GET /v1/drivers/{id}/data-export generates JSON of all personal data for driver subject access request. HMAC-SHA256 signed ETA webhooks: per-partner HMAC secret in Secrets Manager; webhook body signed with X-Signature header; partners verify on receipt. TLS 1.3 at ALB.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ESLint and Vitest for Vue 3 SPA, Jest with 80% coverage for each Fastify service, Docker build per service, push to ECR. ECS rolling update to staging on main merge. Manual promotion gate to production. LaunchDarkly feature flags: FLAG_USE_ORDER_ASSIGNMENT gates order-assignment-svc traffic from monolith; FLAG_USE_ROUTE_OPTIMIZER gates route-optimizer-svc; canary rollout at 5% then 25% then 100% with metric gates (route_optimization_duration_ms p99 <3000ms required before advancing). Fargate task definitions: route-optimizer-svc 2 vCPU 4GB (CPU-intensive), eta-tracker-svc 1 vCPU 2GB, others 0.5 vCPU 1GB. Aurora PostgreSQL multi-AZ with automated daily snapshots, 7-day retention. Fargate autoscaling: route-optimizer-svc on CPU 60%, eta-tracker-svc on Redis SSE connection count custom metric.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each Fargate task with distributed tracing across microservice calls. Custom metrics: route_optimization_duration_ms (p50 p95 p99), google_maps_api_calls_daily (budget tracking toward $2k limit), etas_stream_active_connections (per depot), assignment_queue_depth (pending unassigned orders), stop_completion_rate_hourly. Grafana dashboards for dispatch operations team. PagerDuty alerts: route_optimization_duration_ms p99 >3000ms (P1 -- dispatcher SLA breach), etas_stream_reconnect_rate >20/min (P2 -- SSE instability), google_maps_api_daily_cost >$60 (P3 -- budget warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Projected $3,400/month: ECS Fargate $1,800 (five services), Aurora PostgreSQL $600, ElastiCache Redis $200, CloudFront $100, Google Maps API $750 (estimated 150K Directions API calls/month at current volume with Redis cache reducing calls by 40%), Secrets Manager and misc $150. LaunchDarkly $0 at startup tier. Fargate Savings Plans reduce compute by 30% at committed usage. Google Maps API cost monitored via custom Datadog metric with daily spend alert.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP Dispatch and Routing (12 weeks): order-assignment-svc with PostGIS stop clustering, route-optimizer-svc with Google Maps Directions API and Redis cache, dispatch console Vue 3 SPA with map and order queue, basic ETA SSE stream, Auth0 OIDC integration, LaunchDarkly migration flags. Feature flag at 5% traffic to validate vs monolith. Phase 2 Full Decomposition and Analytics (8 weeks post-MVP): eta-tracker-svc with position-based recomputation, driver-comm-svc with push notifications, analytics-svc with depot performance metrics, GDPR data export endpoint for drivers, HMAC-signed ETA webhooks for integration partners. Phase 3 Advanced Optimization (ongoing): multi-stop traffic-aware reoptimization, recipient-facing ETA tracking page, predictive delivery time model.
target_output.development_guidelines: Vue 3: Composition API only (no Options API), TypeScript strict, ESLint Vue 3 recommended + Prettier, Vitest for component tests. Fastify: no global state in service modules (stateless handlers), Jest with 80% coverage gate for route handlers and service logic. PostGIS queries must use parameterized queries with pg driver; no raw string interpolation for geospatial inputs. Google Maps API key restricted to IP allowlist of Fargate NAT gateway IPs in Cloud Console. All Secrets Manager values accessed via AWS SDK at task startup, never hardcoded. LaunchDarkly flags must have fallback to monolith behavior for graceful degradation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Google Maps API latency spike causes route-optimizer-svc to exceed 3-second SLA. Mitigation: Redis route segment cache serves cached result for driver on same corridor; circuit breaker returns last-cached route with stale flag after 500ms timeout. Risk: SSE connection storms when dispatchers reconnect simultaneously after brief network interruption. Mitigation: jittered reconnection in VueQuery SSE client (500ms to 3000ms random delay); eta-tracker-svc SSE connection count metric triggers Fargate autoscaling. Risk: PostGIS service_zone query slow during peak morning order ingest (500 orders in 10 minutes). Mitigation: spatial index on service_zones.polygon via GIST index; GiST index on drivers current position for ST_Within proximity queries. Tradeoff: five separate Fargate services over a modular monolith increases deployment and operational complexity but enables independent scaling of route-optimizer-svc (CPU-intensive) from lightweight order-assignment-svc.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected PostGIS over Redis geospatial for service zone polygon queries because Redis GEORADIUS does not support arbitrary polygon containment. Chose Google Maps Directions API over OSRM self-hosted to avoid routing engine operational overhead. Confirmed LaunchDarkly for migration feature flags over custom FF implementation for audit trail and canary controls. SSE chosen over WebSocket for ETA streaming because one-directional server push is sufficient and SSE reconnection is handled natively by browsers.

metadata.notes: Last-mile fleet dispatch microservices decomposition on AWS Fargate with Vue 3 and PostGIS; moderate quality with comprehensive architecture but cost_and_scaling section is thinner than ideal.

project_goal.value: Lift historical vehicle inspection records, repair histories, and warranty claims from on-premise SQL Server into Azure Data Lake Gen2 for unified fleet analytics
project_goal.rationale: Legacy SQL Server blocks cross-fleet analytics and BI tooling

target_users.value: Fleet analytics engineers and BI team accessing Synapse Analytics
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are internal data teams running fleet reporting

project_class.rationale: Batch extraction, transformation, and load from SQL Server to Delta Lake

capabilities.rationale: Pipeline is data-centric with scheduled batch jobs and SQL Server integration

complexity_level.rationale: Schema transformation and data quality validation add moderate complexity beyond simple extract-load

risk_level.rationale: Legacy SQL Server schema inconsistencies and referential integrity gaps pose data loss risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Vehicle and repair records are internal operational data, no PII or financial data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline and resulting lake are accessible only within corporate Azure subscription

access_model.value: Azure AD service principal with RBAC on ADLS Gen2 containers and Synapse workspace
access_model.rationale: Service-to-service access via managed identities, no human user credentials in pipeline

feature_scope.value: Full historical extraction of inspection_records, repair_orders, warranty_claims, parts_replacements, service_technicians, vehicle_histories; schema normalization; Delta Lake partitioned storage; data quality gates; Synapse Analytics integration
feature_scope.rationale: Complete migration scope required for analytics team to retire SQL Server dependency

mvp_scope.value: Extraction and Delta Lake loading of inspection_records and vehicle_histories with row-count and checksum validation, supporting initial fleet utilization dashboards
mvp_scope.rationale: Two highest-priority datasets needed for Q2 analytics deliverable

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, ADLS Gen2 encryption at rest with CMK, Azure AD service principal with least-privilege RBAC, no plain-text credentials in pipeline code
security_baseline.rationale: Corporate data governance standard for all internal pipelines

frontend_stack.value: None — no user-facing UI; pipeline outputs to ADLS Gen2 and Synapse
frontend_stack.rationale: Data pipeline with no browser interface

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with dbt Core and Apache Spark on Azure Databricks
backend_stack.rationale: Databricks provides managed Spark with Delta Lake native support; dbt handles transformation layer

data_platform.value: Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 (Delta Lake format) with Azure Synapse Analytics as query layer
data_platform.rationale: ADLS Gen2 + Delta Lake is the corporate standard for analytical workloads on Azure

hosting_target.value: Azure Databricks on Azure in eastus2, orchestrated via Azure DevOps Pipelines
hosting_target.rationale: Databricks manages Spark cluster lifecycle; AzDO provides pipeline scheduling and CI/CD

privacy_retention_policy.value: Migrated records retain original creation timestamps; processed Delta tables stored indefinitely for historical analytics; raw landing zone purged after 90-day validation window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Fleet records have no regulatory minimum retention; raw zone cleared after validation

future_scope.value: Real-time telematics stream ingestion via Azure Event Hubs, predictive maintenance ML models on Synapse Spark
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after historical backfill establishes data lake foundation

constraints.value: SQL Server source is read-only; migration must not impact production OLTP performance; must complete full historical load within 4-week window
constraints.rationale: SQL Server hosts live dispatch operations; read replicas will be used for extraction

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for Databricks job metrics, Azure DevOps pipeline run history, custom data quality metric logging to ADLS audit table
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard on Azure stack

execution_preference.value: Iterative batch releases by entity group with validation gates before proceeding to next entity
execution_preference.rationale: Reduces blast radius if schema normalization issues surface mid-migration

project.goal: Migrate 7 years of fleet inspection and repair records from SQL Server to Delta Lake on ADLS Gen2
project.target_users: Fleet analytics engineers, BI team via Synapse Analytics

backend.framework: dbt Core for transformations, PySpark for extraction and load
backend.orchestration: Azure DevOps Pipelines for scheduling and CI/CD

security.auth: Azure AD service principal, managed identity for Databricks-to-ADLS access
security.encryption: CMK encryption at rest on ADLS Gen2, TLS 1.3 in transit to SQL Server
security.secrets: Azure Key Vault for SQL Server connection strings

data.target_db: ADLS Gen2 Delta Lake, Azure Synapse Analytics external tables
data.quality: Row count checksums, referential integrity checks, value distribution validation

devops.environments: dev (sample dataset), staging (last 6 months), production (full 7-year backfill)

constraints.source_access: Read-only SQL Server replica, max 4 parallel extraction threads

confirmed_decisions.delta_format: Delta Lake v2 on ADLS Gen2

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration scope is well-bounded with clear entity priority order. Starting with inspection_records and vehicle_histories aligns with the Q2 analytics deliverable. The 4-week load window is achievable given batch window extraction.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Databricks Delta Lake is the right choice for this migration given the need for ACID transactions during incremental loads and schema evolution support. Recommend using dbt snapshots for slowly-changing dimension tables like service_technicians and vehicle_histories to capture historical state.
reasoner_reviews.security: ADLS Gen2 with Azure AD managed identity eliminates credential management risk. CMK encryption meets corporate data governance. SQL Server read replica access via service principal prevents any write risk to source.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-week historical load is feasible with parallel extraction across entity groups. Read replica access must be tested for query performance under parallel extraction load before production migration begins.
reasoner_reviews.critic: dbt and Spark running together in Databricks adds operational complexity. Ensure the team has Databricks notebook authoring experience, and that dbt-databricks connector version is pinned to avoid silent breaking changes between Databricks Runtime versions.

backend.service_design: Extraction layer: PySpark notebooks per entity group reading from SQL Server JDBC connector with configurable batch_size and watermark_column parameters. Transformation layer: dbt models organized in staging/intermediate/mart layers. Load: Delta Lake MERGE statements for incremental runs after initial full load.
backend.business_modules: entity_extractor.py (JDBC extraction with pagination), schema_normalizer.py (legacy field name mapping and ENUM standardization), quality_checker.py (checksum comparison and distribution validation), dq_reporter.py (writes quality metrics to audit Delta table)
backend.background_jobs: Azure DevOps scheduled pipeline triggers Databricks Job with three tasks: (1) extract-and-land to raw zone, (2) dbt run for staging and intermediate models, (3) quality gate check with pass/fail notification
backend.failure_handling: Pipeline halts on quality gate failure with Azure Monitor alert; failed batch window recorded to pipeline_runs table; supports re-run with same watermark bounds after source issue resolved

security.auth_design: Databricks cluster uses Azure AD passthrough with managed identity; SQL Server access via service principal credentials stored in Azure Key Vault; dbt profiles reference Key Vault secrets, never plain-text
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault stores SQL Server connection string and CMK reference; Key Vault access policy scoped to Databricks service principal; secret rotation policy 90 days
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All Databricks job runs logged to Azure Monitor; data quality results written to audit_events Delta table with run_id, entity, rows_extracted, rows_loaded, checksum_match, timestamp; ADLS access logs via Azure Diagnostic Settings

data.entities: raw_inspection_records (landing zone, exact SQL Server schema), staging.inspection_records (normalized field names, enum standardization), mart.inspection_records (final analytics model with partition by inspection_date year/month). Same pattern for repair_orders, warranty_claims, parts_replacements, service_technicians, vehicle_histories.
data.storage_design: ADLS Gen2 containers: raw/ (landing, 90-day retention), processed/ (Delta Lake tables, permanent), audit/ (quality metrics and pipeline runs). Delta tables partitioned by entity and date range for Synapse query performance.
data.schema_notes: Legacy SQL Server uses inconsistent naming (InspVehicleID vs vehicle_id vs VehID across tables); dbt staging models apply unified snake_case column naming and standardize status ENUMs (e.g., 'P','PASS','1' all map to inspection_status='passed')
data.migration_notes: Initial full load via PySpark JDBC bulk read; subsequent runs use dbt snapshots with updated_at watermark column; referential integrity validated post-load by joining repair_orders to vehicle_histories on vin and asserting zero orphan records

devops.deployment_topology: Azure DevOps pipeline triggers Databricks Jobs API; Databricks cluster auto-terminates after job completion to control cost; dbt models deployed via dbt-databricks adapter
devops.environments: dev (500-record sample per entity), staging (last 6 months, validates transformation logic), production (7-year full backfill, parallelized across entity groups)
devops.ci_cd_design: PR to main runs dbt compile and dbt test on dev cluster; merge to main triggers staging run with data quality gate; manual approval required before production pipeline executes
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor dashboards for job duration, rows processed, and DQ pass rate; Azure DevOps release annotations on Databricks job history; PagerDuty alert on quality gate failure or job duration >6 hours

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a batch migration pipeline to lift 7 years of fleet inspection and repair data from on-premise SQL Server into Azure Delta Lake. Key decisions: Databricks Spark for scalable JDBC extraction, dbt for typed transformation with staging/mart layers, and mandatory data quality gates between each phase to catch schema normalization failures before they propagate to analytics tables. The read-only replica constraint and 4-week window drive a parallel entity-group extraction strategy.
target_output.title: Fleet Records Migration Pipeline to Azure Delta Lake
target_output.executive_summary: A PySpark and dbt-based batch migration pipeline running on Azure Databricks that extracts 7 years of vehicle inspection records, repair orders, warranty claims, and related entities from on-premise SQL Server into partitioned Delta Lake tables on ADLS Gen2. Data quality gates validate row counts and referential integrity between extraction and promotion stages. Azure Synapse Analytics external tables provide immediate query access to migrated data for the fleet analytics team.
target_output.architecture_overview: ELT pipeline architecture with three stages: (1) Extract — PySpark JDBC jobs read SQL Server read replica in configurable batch windows and land raw Parquet snapshots to ADLS Gen2 raw/ container. (2) Transform — dbt Core on Databricks processes staging models (schema normalization, ENUM standardization) and mart models (analytics-optimized partitioned Delta tables). (3) Quality Gate — automated checks compare source row counts, validate referential integrity across vehicle_histories and repair_orders, and assert value distribution thresholds before marking each entity batch as promoted. Azure DevOps Pipelines orchestrate the full sequence; Azure AD managed identity governs all service-to-service access.
target_output.technology_stack: Extraction: Python 3.12, PySpark on Databricks Runtime 14.x, SQL Server JDBC driver. Transformation: dbt Core 1.7 with dbt-databricks adapter, Jinja2 macros for ENUM mapping. Storage: ADLS Gen2 with Delta Lake v2, Azure Synapse Analytics (external tables over Delta). Orchestration: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Security: Azure Key Vault for secrets, Azure AD managed identity. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Azure DevOps pipeline run history.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Entity Extraction Module: configurable batch_size and watermark parameters per entity, supports full-load and incremental modes. Schema Normalization: dbt staging models map 23 legacy field name variants to unified snake_case schema and standardize 6 status ENUM inconsistencies across inspection_records and repair_orders. Data Quality Engine: validates row counts against SQL Server source checksums, referential integrity between repair_orders and vehicle_histories, and value distribution tolerances. Audit Trail: every pipeline run writes metrics to audit_events Delta table queryable from Synapse. Synapse Integration: external tables and views for analytics team access.
target_output.system_components: SQL Server 2019 read replica (source), Azure Databricks cluster (PySpark extraction and Spark-based dbt execution), ADLS Gen2 raw/ container (landing zone, 90-day TTL), ADLS Gen2 processed/ container (Delta Lake permanent store), Azure Synapse Analytics workspace (query layer with external tables), Azure Key Vault (SQL Server and CMK secrets), Azure DevOps Pipelines (orchestration and CI/CD), Azure Monitor (job and DQ metrics).
target_output.workflows: Entity Extraction Workflow: AzDO pipeline triggers Databricks Job; entity_extractor.py connects to SQL Server read replica via JDBC with service principal credentials from Key Vault; reads inspection_records in 10K-row batches using inspection_date watermark; writes Parquet files to raw/inspection_records/year=YYYY/month=MM/; job completes with row count logged to audit_events. Data Quality Gate Workflow: quality_checker.py queries SQL Server source for COUNT(*) and MD5 checksum on key columns; compares to landed Parquet row count and column checksums; on match, promotes entity to processed/ Delta table via dbt run; on mismatch >0.01%, writes failure record to audit_events and sends Azure Monitor alert, halting pipeline for that entity group.
target_output.data_model: raw_inspection_records (InspID, VehicleID, InspDate, InspType, InspectorID, Result, Notes — exact SQL Server schema preserved). staging.inspection_records (inspection_id, vehicle_id, inspection_date, inspection_type ENUM annual/roadside/pre_trip, inspector_technician_id, result ENUM passed/failed/conditional, notes, _extracted_at). mart.inspection_records (all staging columns, partitioned by inspection_year INT and inspection_month INT, _dbt_updated_at). repair_orders (repair_order_id, vehicle_id, open_date, close_date, repair_category, labor_hours DECIMAL, parts_cost DECIMAL, technician_id, warranty_claim_id FK nullable). warranty_claims (warranty_claim_id, vehicle_id, vin, claim_date, component_code, failure_description, approved_amount DECIMAL, status ENUM submitted/approved/denied). parts_replacements (replacement_id, repair_order_id, part_number, part_description, quantity, unit_cost). service_technicians (technician_id, name, certification_level, hire_date). vehicle_histories (vin, make, model, year, fleet_id, acquisition_date, disposal_date nullable, odometer_at_migration).
target_output.api_design: No external REST API. Internal programmatic interfaces: entity_extractor.extract(entity_name, watermark_start, watermark_end, batch_size) -> DataFrame. quality_checker.validate(entity_name, run_id) -> QualityResult(passed, source_count, landed_count, checksum_match). dbt CLI: dbt run --select staging.inspection_records+ for entity-scoped runs. Databricks Jobs API used by AzDO for job submission: POST /api/2.1/jobs/runs/submit with notebook_params including entity_name and run_mode.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD service principal with Storage Blob Data Contributor on processed/ container and Storage Blob Data Reader on raw/; SQL Server access via read-only login, credentials in Key Vault with 90-day rotation.
