previous_plan.title: Fleet Telematics SaaS Landing Page Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 landing page with Contentful CMS integration, Vercel deployment, and GA4 analytics. CMS-editable hero, feature highlights, pricing tiers, and customer logos with lead capture form forwarding to CRM.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: CRM API key in Vercel environment variables. GA4 loaded via next/script afterInteractive — loads unconditionally on page load before cookie consent banner is shown (identified as GDPR risk in round 1 audit).
previous_plan.workflows: Marketing Publish: editor updates pricing copy in Contentful, publishes; Contentful webhook triggers Vercel revalidation; updated pricing visible within 60 seconds. Lead Capture: visitor fills form, submits to /api/lead serverless function, function forwards to CRM API with UTM source. Cookie Consent: banner shown on first visit but GA4 already loaded before interaction.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round 2 for a simple Next.js landing page. The single fix needed is straightforward: move GA4 initialization to be conditional on cookie consent state. Will implement a consent context that persists to localStorage, dynamically import GA4 only when consent is 'granted', and use gtag consent mode v2 to signal denied state without loading the full library. Architecture is otherwise appropriate for a landing_page project class.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced unconditional next/script GA4 component with a ConsentAwareAnalytics component that reads consent state from localStorage on mount; if 'analytics_consent' is 'granted', dynamically loads GA4 via gtag.js; if not yet set, initializes gtag with consent mode v2 defaults (denied) and upgrades to granted after user accepts the cookie banner. Consent state persisted to localStorage on banner interaction and re-checked on each page navigation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GA4 data collection does not begin for EU visitors until they explicitly accept the cookie banner; gtag consent mode v2 enables Google to model conversion data for denied-consent users without collecting raw PII

target_output.title: Fleet Telematics SaaS Landing Page Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 landing page with Contentful headless CMS, Vercel deployment, and GDPR-compliant GA4 analytics for a fleet telematics SaaS startup. Marketing editors update hero copy, feature highlights, and pricing tiers via Contentful without engineering involvement; content deploys via Contentful webhook triggering Vercel ISR revalidation. Version 2 fixes the GDPR consent violation by making GA4 conditional on cookie consent state using gtag consent mode v2.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 app router with static generation and 60-second ISR revalidation fetching content from Contentful Delivery API at build and revalidation time. Vercel hosts the site globally with CDN and automatic HTTPS. A single Vercel serverless function at /api/lead handles lead form submissions and forwards to the CRM API, keeping the CRM key server-side. GA4 analytics are initialized via a ConsentAwareAnalytics component that checks localStorage for consent state before loading gtag.js; gtag consent mode v2 is initialized with denied defaults on first visit, upgraded to granted after cookie banner acceptance.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Next.js 14 with TypeScript. Styling: Tailwind CSS. CMS: Contentful Delivery API with ISR revalidation. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with gtag consent mode v2 via ConsentAwareAnalytics component. Hosting: Vercel with preview deployments per PR. Lead forwarding: Vercel serverless function calling CRM REST API. CI: GitHub Actions for type-check and lint.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Hero Section: Contentful-editable headline, subheadline, CTA button label and URL, background image. Feature Highlights: grid of CMS-managed cards with GPS tracking and route optimization feature descriptions, icons, and sort order. Pricing Table: Contentful-managed tier names, monthly/annual prices, feature lists, and featured tier flag. Customer Logo Strip: CMS-managed logo images with alt text for accessibility. Lead Capture Form: name, company, email fields submitting to /api/lead with UTM parameter capture. Cookie Consent Banner: shown on first visit, persists acceptance to localStorage, triggers GA4 load on accept. ConsentAwareAnalytics: initializes gtag consent mode v2 with denied defaults; upgrades to granted on banner acceptance; fires lead_form_submitted and pricing_tier_clicked events only after consent.
target_output.system_components: Next.js app router with pages component tree. Contentful Delivery API client fetching content at build and ISR revalidation. ConsentAwareAnalytics React component managing gtag consent mode v2 lifecycle. CookieConsentBanner component with localStorage persistence. Vercel serverless function /api/lead with CRM API key from environment variables. Vercel CDN serving static assets globally. Contentful webhook calling Vercel deploy hook URL on content publish.
target_output.workflows: Marketing Content Update: editor updates hero headline or pricing tier in Contentful and publishes; Contentful webhook fires to Vercel deploy hook URL; Vercel triggers ISR revalidation of affected pages; updated content visible globally within 60 seconds without a full rebuild. Lead Capture: visitor fills name, company, email in lead form; submits to POST /api/lead; serverless function appends UTM parameters from query string and forwards to CRM API; CRM confirms lead created; thank-you message shown. Cookie Consent: first-time EU visitor sees cookie banner; ConsentAwareAnalytics initializes gtag with consent mode v2 defaults (ad_storage: denied, analytics_storage: denied); visitor clicks Accept; consent state written to localStorage analytics_consent='granted'; gtag.js loaded dynamically; GA4 begins firing page_view and conversion events.
target_output.data_model: Contentful content models: hero_section (headline TEXT, subheadline TEXT, cta_label TEXT, cta_url TEXT, background_image Asset). feature_highlight (title TEXT, description TEXT, icon Asset, sort_order INT). pricing_tier (name TEXT, monthly_price INT, annual_price INT, features ARRAY[TEXT], is_featured BOOL). customer_logo (company_name TEXT, logo_image Asset, alt_text TEXT). lead_capture_form (form_id TEXT, crm_list_id TEXT). No application database. localStorage key: analytics_consent with values granted/denied. Lead data forwarded to CRM with no local retention.
target_output.api_design: POST /api/lead accepts {name: string, company: string, email: string} plus query parameters utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign; validates required fields; forwards to CRM API POST /contacts with Authorization header from VERCEL_CRM_API_KEY env var; returns 200 OK or 422 validation error. Contentful Delivery API: GET /spaces/{space_id}/entries?content_type={type} called server-side during Next.js build and ISR revalidation. Vercel deploy hook: POST webhook URL triggered by Contentful on content publish. No client-side API calls except GA4 gtag after consent.
target_output.security_and_compliance: CRM API key stored in Vercel environment variable VERCEL_CRM_API_KEY; never included in client bundle; only accessed in /api/lead serverless function. Contentful delivery token in Vercel environment variable; read-only delivery API token safe for server-side use. GA4 consent mode v2 fix: gtag initialized with ad_storage: denied, analytics_storage: denied defaults; upgraded to granted only after explicit cookie banner acceptance; consent state persisted to localStorage. GDPR compliance: no analytics data collected before consent; lead form data forwarded immediately to CRM with no local storage. HTTPS enforced via Vercel on all routes. CSP header configured in next.config.js.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions on PR: tsc --noEmit type check, eslint --max-warnings 0, next build dry run. Vercel auto-deploys preview on every PR push; preview URL posted to PR for marketing review. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deploy. Contentful webhook URL configured in Contentful space settings pointing to Vercel revalidation endpoint; triggers on publish of any content type. Rollback: Vercel dashboard instant rollback to any prior deployment. No server infrastructure to manage. Secrets rotation: update Vercel environment variable and redeploy.
target_output.observability: GA4 events (consent-gated): page_view with page_title and page_path, lead_form_submitted with utm_source/utm_medium/utm_campaign, pricing_tier_clicked with tier_name. Vercel Analytics dashboard: Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID) per page. Vercel function logs for /api/lead: request count, CRM API response codes, and error rates. No custom alerting needed for a landing page. Marketing monitors GA4 Conversions report for lead_form_submitted event volume by UTM source.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel hobby or pro plan sufficient for landing page traffic. Contentful free tier covers 25K API calls/month and 5 editors. GA4 is free. Total cost under $20/month. Scaling is automatic via Vercel CDN — no manual scaling needed. /api/lead serverless function scales to Vercel's concurrency limits (1000 concurrent executions on pro) with no configuration.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v1 (completed): Next.js landing page with Contentful ISR, hero/features/pricing/logos, lead form, Vercel deployment, GA4 (with consent flaw). Phase 2 v2 (current): ConsentAwareAnalytics with gtag consent mode v2, CookieConsentBanner with localStorage persistence, consent-gated event tracking. Phase 3: A/B testing for hero copy variants using Vercel Edge Config, blog section with Contentful blog content model for SEO, localized content for DE/FR EU markets.
target_output.development_guidelines: ConsentAwareAnalytics component must be tested with consent granted and denied states. GA4 gtag.js must not appear in network requests when consent is denied — verify in integration test. Lead form /api/lead must return 422 for missing required fields and not forward incomplete leads to CRM. Contentful content model changes require schema migration tested against staging environment. Tailwind CSS purge must be configured to remove unused classes in production build to keep CSS bundle under 10 KB.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Contentful ISR 60-second delay means pricing changes take up to 60 seconds to appear globally; marketing team must account for this in time-sensitive campaigns. Mitigation: document the delay in the marketing runbook; option to trigger manual Vercel revalidation for urgent changes. Risk: gtag consent mode v2 modeling data quality is lower than full analytics for denied-consent users. Tradeoff: GDPR compliance requires accepting reduced analytics fidelity for EU traffic as a necessary cost of operating in regulated markets.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed gtag consent mode v2 over a full consent management platform (CMP); CMP adds complexity and cost not warranted for a single-language landing page. Confirmed Contentful ISR over a full rebuild on every publish because rebuild time is 45 seconds and ISR achieves the same result in 60 seconds without blocking the build pipeline. Confirmed localStorage over cookie for consent state persistence because cookie consent banner sets the precedent; using localStorage avoids the irony of a cookie for consent management.

metadata.notes: Round 2 landing page revision fixing GDPR consent violation for GA4 analytics; Next.js/Contentful/Vercel fleet telematics SaaS site. Weak quality: some sections at minimum depth appropriate for simple landing page.

profile.domain: Construction Equipment Fuel Waste Analytics Pipeline

project_goal.value: Batch data pipeline analyzing idle engine time, suboptimal routing, and excessive RPM events across a construction equipment fleet to quantify fuel waste and generate actionable savings reports for site supervisors
project_goal.rationale: Fleet operations wants quantified fuel cost savings opportunities to guide equipment operator training and dispatch decisions

target_users.value: Site supervisors receiving weekly email reports and fleet managers accessing Snowflake dashboards for trend analysis
target_users.rationale: Two consumer tiers: operational supervisors need actionable weekly summaries, fleet managers need historical trend data

project_class.rationale: Batch processing pipeline with no user-facing application component

capabilities.rationale: Data transformation, scheduled batch jobs, and analytics output are the three capability pillars

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-stage Airflow DAGs with Snowflake transformations and PDF report generation add moderate orchestration complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal operational data; no PII, no financial data, no external exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Equipment telemetry and fuel cost data is internal operational data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs internally; reports distributed to internal email only

access_model.value: IAM roles for MWAA and Snowflake; site supervisors receive PDF reports via email; fleet managers access Snowflake via BI tool with read-only role
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access appropriate for internal analytics pipeline

feature_scope.value: Daily idle time aggregation per equipment unit, RPM distribution profile computation, fuel waste cost estimation using OEM fuel curves and diesel price inputs, weekly savings report generation ranking equipment by opportunity, email distribution to site supervisors
feature_scope.rationale: Complete pipeline from raw telemetry to actionable weekly reports

mvp_scope.value: Daily idle time aggregation and fuel waste cost estimation per equipment unit; weekly email report with top-10 opportunity ranking for supervisors
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable output to demonstrate fuel savings opportunity before investing in full RPM analysis

security_baseline.value: IAM roles for MWAA task execution and Snowflake access; Snowflake network policy restricting connections to VPC CIDR; no Snowflake credentials in plaintext
security_baseline.rationale: Internal pipeline; primary controls are IAM and network policy

frontend_stack.value: None — batch pipeline with email report output; no web UI
frontend_stack.rationale: No user-facing interface; output is PDF reports via email and Snowflake tables

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow on MWAA; WeasyPrint for PDF generation
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow is the team standard for batch orchestration; WeasyPrint generates PDF reports from Jinja2 HTML templates

data_platform.value: Snowflake as the analytical warehouse; raw telemetry ingested to staging schema, transformed via dbt models to analytics schema
data_platform.rationale: Snowflake provides cost-efficient storage and separation of compute from storage for batch analytics

hosting_target.value: AWS MWAA (Managed Apache Airflow) with Snowflake as external warehouse
hosting_target.rationale: MWAA eliminates Airflow infrastructure management; Snowflake handles warehouse scaling

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw equipment telemetry retained for 1 year in Snowflake staging; aggregated fuel waste summaries retained for 3 years; PDF reports retained in S3 for 2 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Operational data retention aligned with fleet contract audit requirements

future_scope.value: Predictive maintenance alerts based on RPM anomaly patterns, route optimization recommendations integrating with dispatch system, real-time dashboard for fleet managers
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 capabilities building on the batch analytics foundation

constraints.value: Snowflake warehouse must be suspended when not in use to control costs; pipeline must complete daily aggregation within 2-hour window between midnight and 02:00 local time; diesel price input updated weekly by fleet manager
constraints.rationale: Cost control and operational timing requirements for overnight batch processing

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for MWAA task durations and failure alerts; Airflow task success/failure metrics; email alert to pipeline owner on DAG failure
observability_baseline.rationale: Operational monitoring for overnight batch pipeline with email escalation on failure

execution_preference.value: dbt models for Snowflake transformations under version control; Airflow DAGs in GitLab CI pipeline; weekly diesel price updated via Airflow Variable UI
execution_preference.rationale: Code-first approach with managed data inputs for operational flexibility

project.goal: Fuel waste analytics pipeline for construction equipment fleet
project.target_users: Site supervisors and fleet managers

backend.framework: Apache Airflow on MWAA, dbt for Snowflake transformations, WeasyPrint for PDF generation

security.auth: IAM roles for MWAA; Snowflake key pair authentication

devops.ci: GitLab CI for dbt model tests and DAG lint
devops.environments: dev Snowflake database, production Snowflake database

constraints.warehouse_cost: Snowflake warehouse auto-suspend after 5 minutes idle

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope is appropriate. The unresolved issue from prior rounds is that the OEM fuel consumption curves are hardcoded as constants in the dbt model SQL rather than stored as a versioned reference table. This means updating fuel curves for new equipment models requires a code change and deployment, which is operationally burdensome and error-prone.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Moving the OEM fuel curves to a Snowflake reference table is the right architectural fix. It decouples equipment model data from transformation logic, allows fleet managers to add new equipment models without engineering involvement, and makes curves auditable with version history. The dbt model should JOIN against the reference table rather than use hardcoded CASE expressions.
reasoner_reviews.security: No security concerns remaining after round 2 fixes. IAM roles and Snowflake network policy are correctly configured. The fuel curve reference table access should be read-only for the MWAA execution role.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Snowflake warehouse auto-suspend after 5 minutes idle is already implemented. Moving fuel curves to a reference table adds one additional Snowflake query per daily DAG run but does not materially affect the 2-hour processing window.

backend.service_design: Three Airflow DAGs: fuel_waste_daily (runs at 00:30 UTC, aggregates idle events and RPM profiles, computes fuel waste estimates joining against oem_fuel_curves reference table), savings_report_weekly (runs Sunday 04:00 UTC, generates per-site PDF reports and sends via SES), diesel_price_update (manual trigger DAG, updates DIESEL_PRICE_USD Airflow Variable)
backend.business_modules: dbt project fuel_analytics with models: stg_equipment_events (staging), int_idle_summaries (idle duration aggregation per equipment unit per day), int_rpm_profiles (RPM distribution percentiles per unit per day), oem_fuel_curves (reference seed table), fct_fuel_waste_daily (joins int models against oem_fuel_curves and DIESEL_PRICE_USD variable), fct_savings_opportunities (weekly rank of units by estimated dollar waste)
backend.background_jobs: fuel_waste_daily DAG: task_validate_sources checks row counts in staging, task_run_dbt_daily runs dbt run --select fct_fuel_waste_daily, task_assert_quality runs dbt test; savings_report_weekly DAG: task_render_pdf generates WeasyPrint PDF from Jinja2 template, task_send_ses emails PDF to supervisor list
backend.failure_handling: DAG failure triggers email alert via Airflow SLA miss callback; failed task retries 2 times with 10-minute delay; task_validate_sources failure aborts downstream tasks via Airflow upstream_failed state to prevent corrupt reports

security.auth_design: MWAA execution role IAM policy grants Snowflake connection via Secrets Manager; Snowflake key pair authentication with private key in AWS Secrets Manager
security.secrets_management: Snowflake private key stored in AWS Secrets Manager; SES sending credentials via MWAA execution IAM role; no credentials in DAG code or environment variables
security.security_risks: oem_fuel_curves reference table must be restricted to read-only for pipeline execution role; only fleet data engineers should have INSERT/UPDATE access to prevent unauthorized curve manipulation affecting savings calculations

data.entities: equipment_units (unit_id, serial_number, make, model, equipment_class ENUM excavator/crane/bulldozer/grader, site_id, commissioned_date), idle_events (event_id, unit_id, start_time, end_time, idle_duration_minutes, engine_temp_c, ambient_temp_c), rpm_profiles (profile_id, unit_id, date, rpm_p50, rpm_p90, rpm_p99, rpm_over_2500_pct, sample_count), fuel_consumption_logs (log_id, unit_id, date, fuel_liters_consumed, source ENUM telematics/manual_fill), route_segments (segment_id, unit_id, start_coords, end_coords, distance_km, duration_minutes, date), savings_reports (report_id, site_id, report_week, generated_at, pdf_s3_key, recipient_emails[]), cost_centers (center_id, site_id, name, diesel_price_override_usd)
data.storage_design: Snowflake: staging schema for raw telemetry, analytics schema for dbt models, reference schema for oem_fuel_curves seed table with make/model/fuel_liters_per_hour_at_idle and fuel_liters_per_hour_at_rated_rpm. S3 bucket for PDF report archive with lifecycle policy deleting objects after 2 years
data.analytics_events: dbt model run duration, row counts per model, fuel_waste_daily total estimated waste per site, weekly top-10 unit IDs by dollar waste opportunity — all logged to CloudWatch via Airflow metrics

devops.deployment_topology: MWAA environment in VPC with Snowflake connection via PrivateLink; S3 bucket for MWAA DAG files and PDF reports; Secrets Manager for Snowflake credentials
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: dbt compile --profiles-dir dev, dbt test --select staging+, sqlfluff lint dbt/models/, DAG syntax validation via python -c import dag_file; pipeline blocks merge if dbt tests fail
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch metrics for MWAA task duration and failure counts; Airflow email on SLA miss; weekly report delivery confirmation logged to CloudWatch
devops.devops_risks: MWAA version upgrades require testing all DAGs against new Airflow version; pin MWAA to major Airflow version and schedule upgrades quarterly

ISS-001.title: OEM fuel consumption curves hardcoded in dbt SQL as CASE expressions
ISS-001.detail: The fct_fuel_waste_daily dbt model computes fuel consumption estimates using CASE expressions hardcoded with OEM values for each equipment make/model. Adding a new equipment model (e.g., a new excavator variant) requires modifying the dbt SQL and triggering a GitLab CI deployment. Fleet managers cannot independently add equipment models, and the curves are not auditable as data.

history.note: Identified during architecture review; OEM curves baked into SQL
Fix deferred due to scope; still present in round 2 plan

ISS-002.title: Weekly report does not include site-level diesel price override
ISS-002.detail: Resolved in round 2: cost_centers table now includes diesel_price_override_usd column; fct_fuel_waste_daily JOINs against cost_centers and uses override price when non-null, falling back to global DIESEL_PRICE_USD Airflow Variable.

history.note: Sites with different fuel contracts need site-specific diesel prices
cost_centers diesel_price_override_usd added and JOIN implemented in fct_fuel_waste_daily

focus_issues.title: OEM fuel consumption curves hardcoded in dbt SQL as CASE expressions
focus_issues.detail: Hardcoded CASE expressions prevent fleet managers from adding new equipment models without an engineering deployment.

latest_recommendations: Extract OEM fuel curves into a Snowflake seed reference table oem_fuel_curves(make, model, equipment_class, idle_liters_per_hour, rated_rpm_liters_per_hour)
Rewrite fct_fuel_waste_daily to JOIN against oem_fuel_curves reference table instead of CASE expressions
Provide a simple CSV template for fleet managers to add new equipment models via dbt seed

previous_plan.title: Construction Equipment Fuel Waste Analytics Pipeline Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python/Airflow/dbt pipeline on MWAA aggregating construction equipment idle time and RPM profiles in Snowflake to estimate fuel waste costs per unit and generate weekly PDF savings reports for site supervisors. Round 2 added per-site diesel price override via cost_centers table.
previous_plan.data_model: Snowflake tables: equipment_units, idle_events, rpm_profiles, fuel_consumption_logs, route_segments, savings_reports, cost_centers (with diesel_price_override_usd added in round 2). fct_fuel_waste_daily uses CASE WHEN make='Caterpillar' AND model='320' THEN 12.5 ... END for OEM fuel curves (ISS-001: still hardcoded).
previous_plan.workflows: Daily fuel_waste_daily DAG aggregates idle_events and rpm_profiles, applies hardcoded CASE OEM fuel curves to estimate liters wasted, multiplies by site diesel price from cost_centers or global DIESEL_PRICE_USD variable. Weekly savings_report_weekly DAG ranks equipment_units by estimated dollar waste and generates PDF per site.
previous_plan.risks_and_tradeoffs: ISS-001 remains: adding a new excavator model to the fleet requires modifying fct_fuel_waste_daily CASE expression and triggering GitLab CI deployment. ISS-002 resolved.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for a construction equipment fuel waste pipeline. Single focus issue: extract OEM fuel consumption curves from hardcoded CASE expressions in the fct_fuel_waste_daily dbt model into a proper seed reference table. This is a data architecture fix — create oem_fuel_curves as a dbt seed CSV (make, model, equipment_class, idle_liters_per_hour, rated_rpm_liters_per_hour), update fct_fuel_waste_daily to JOIN against it, and document the fleet manager process for adding new equipment models via GitLab MR. Plan quality is weak per distribution rules for this slot but technically correct.

fix_report.action_taken: Extracted OEM fuel consumption values from CASE expressions in fct_fuel_waste_daily into a dbt seed file seeds/oem_fuel_curves.csv with columns: make, model, equipment_class, idle_liters_per_hour, rated_rpm_liters_per_hour. Rewrote fct_fuel_waste_daily to JOIN against ref('oem_fuel_curves') on equipment_units.make and equipment_units.model. Added a dbt singular test asserting no equipment_units rows exist without a matching oem_fuel_curves entry. Documented fleet manager workflow for adding new equipment models via a GitLab MR updating the seed CSV file.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Fleet managers can add new equipment models by submitting a GitLab MR editing oem_fuel_curves.csv with no SQL knowledge required; dbt CI tests validate curve coverage before merge; fuel waste estimates for new models become available in the next daily DAG run after merge

target_output.title: Construction Equipment Fuel Waste Analytics Pipeline Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: Python/Airflow/dbt batch pipeline on AWS MWAA aggregating idle engine time and RPM distribution profiles for construction equipment in Snowflake to estimate fuel waste and produce weekly PDF savings reports for site supervisors. Version 3 resolves the OEM fuel curve hardcoding by migrating to a dbt seed reference table, enabling fleet managers to add new equipment models via GitLab MR without SQL knowledge.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-DAG Airflow pipeline on MWAA: fuel_waste_daily runs nightly aggregating idle_events and rpm_profiles from Snowflake staging schema into dbt intermediate models, then computing fct_fuel_waste_daily by JOINing against the oem_fuel_curves seed reference table. savings_report_weekly runs Sunday morning generating ranked PDF reports via WeasyPrint and distributing to supervisors via SES. diesel_price_update is a manually triggered DAG for updating the global diesel price Airflow Variable. Snowflake warehouse FUEL_ANALYTICS_WH auto-suspends after 5 minutes idle to control costs.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on AWS MWAA. Transformations: dbt 1.7 with Snowflake adapter. Warehouse: Snowflake with FUEL_ANALYTICS_WH. PDF Generation: WeasyPrint 60 with Jinja2 HTML templates. Email Delivery: AWS SES via MWAA execution IAM role. Version Control: GitLab with CI pipeline for dbt tests and DAG validation. Infrastructure: MWAA in VPC with Snowflake PrivateLink.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Daily Idle Aggregation: Airflow task runs dbt model int_idle_summaries aggregating idle_events per equipment unit per day with duration totals. Daily RPM Profiling: dbt model int_rpm_profiles computes p50/p90/p99 RPM percentiles and over-2500-RPM percentage per unit per day. Fuel Waste Estimation: fct_fuel_waste_daily JOINs int_idle_summaries against oem_fuel_curves seed table on make/model to get idle_liters_per_hour; multiplies by idle hours to get wasted liters; multiplies by site diesel price from cost_centers. Weekly Savings Report: fct_savings_opportunities ranks equipment_units by estimated weekly dollar waste; WeasyPrint renders ranked table with recommended actions to PDF; SES sends to site supervisor email list.
target_output.system_components: MWAA Airflow environment hosting three DAGs. Snowflake FUEL_ANALYTICS_WH with staging, analytics, and reference schemas. dbt project with seed, staging, intermediate, and fact model layers. oem_fuel_curves dbt seed CSV as version-controlled reference data. WeasyPrint PDF renderer running in MWAA task. SES for report email delivery. S3 bucket for DAG files, PDF report archive, and dbt seed files. AWS Secrets Manager for Snowflake key pair credentials.
target_output.workflows: Daily Fuel Waste Processing: fuel_waste_daily DAG triggers at 00:30 UTC; task_validate_sources checks row counts in staging schema for completeness; task_run_dbt_daily executes dbt run selecting int_idle_summaries, int_rpm_profiles, fct_fuel_waste_daily; fct_fuel_waste_daily JOINs equipment_units to oem_fuel_curves reference table on (make, model) — unmatched units flagged in a LEFT JOIN warning log rather than silently producing NULL estimates; task_assert_quality runs dbt tests including the singular test asserting full oem_fuel_curves coverage. Weekly Report Generation: savings_report_weekly DAG triggers Sunday 04:00 UTC; queries fct_savings_opportunities ranking equipment_units by estimated weekly_fuel_cost_waste_usd descending; for each site with at least one unit in the top-50, renders a PDF with unit ID, equipment class, estimated waste in liters and USD, and recommended action (idle limit enforcement or route adjustment); SES sends PDF to cost_centers.supervisor_emails for that site.
target_output.data_model: equipment_units (unit_id UUID PK, serial_number VARCHAR, make VARCHAR, model VARCHAR, equipment_class ENUM excavator/crane/bulldozer/grader, site_id UUID FK, commissioned_date DATE). idle_events (event_id UUID PK, unit_id FK, start_time TIMESTAMPTZ, end_time TIMESTAMPTZ, idle_duration_minutes INT, engine_temp_c SMALLINT). rpm_profiles (profile_id UUID PK, unit_id FK, date DATE, rpm_p50 INT, rpm_p90 INT, rpm_p99 INT, rpm_over_2500_pct DECIMAL). fuel_consumption_logs (log_id UUID PK, unit_id FK, date DATE, fuel_liters_consumed DECIMAL, source ENUM telematics/manual_fill). route_segments (segment_id UUID PK, unit_id FK, date DATE, distance_km DECIMAL, duration_minutes INT). savings_reports (report_id UUID PK, site_id FK, report_week DATE, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_s3_key VARCHAR, recipient_count INT). cost_centers (center_id UUID PK, site_id FK, name VARCHAR, diesel_price_override_usd DECIMAL). oem_fuel_curves (make VARCHAR PK, model VARCHAR PK, equipment_class VARCHAR, idle_liters_per_hour DECIMAL, rated_rpm_liters_per_hour DECIMAL) — dbt seed CSV under version control.
target_output.api_design: No external API. Internal interfaces: Airflow KubernetesPodOperator invokes dbt via subprocess in MWAA task; Snowflake Python connector uses key pair authentication from Secrets Manager. PDF reports delivered via SES API call from MWAA execution role. Airflow REST API accessible internally on MWAA for manual DAG triggers. Snowflake PrivateLink endpoint for MWAA-to-Snowflake connections without public internet traversal.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Snowflake key pair private key stored in AWS Secrets Manager; retrieved by MWAA execution role at runtime; not present in DAG code or environment variables. Snowflake network policy restricts FUEL_ANALYTICS_WH connections to MWAA VPC CIDR. oem_fuel_curves reference table restricted to SELECT for pipeline execution role; INSERT/UPDATE reserved for fleet_data_engineer Snowflake role. SES sending domain verified with DKIM. S3 PDF archive bucket with server-side encryption and bucket policy allowing only MWAA execution role and engineering team.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DAG files deployed to MWAA via S3 sync from GitLab CI pipeline on merge to main. dbt project compiled and tested in GitLab CI before merge: dbt compile, dbt test --select staging+. DAG syntax validated via python -c import. MWAA scheduler picks up new DAG files within 30 seconds of S3 sync. oem_fuel_curves.csv changes trigger dbt seed upload and table refresh in next daily run. Snowflake warehouse auto-suspends after 5-minute idle to contain compute costs. Environments: dev uses FUEL_ANALYTICS_DEV_WH Snowflake database for dbt CI testing; production uses FUEL_ANALYTICS_WH.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch custom metrics from Airflow: dag_run_duration_seconds per DAG, task_failure_count per task, dbt_model_row_count per model run. CloudWatch alarm: task_failure_count > 0 triggers email alert to pipeline-owner@fleet.internal. Airflow SLA miss callback for fuel_waste_daily if not complete by 02:00 UTC. Weekly report delivery confirmation logged to CloudWatch log group /mwaa/savings-reports with recipient count and S3 PDF key per site. No external monitoring needed for an internal batch pipeline.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: MWAA small environment: approximately $300/month. Snowflake X-Small warehouse with auto-suspend: approximately $50/month at typical 2-hour daily runtime. S3 PDF storage minimal at fleet scale. Total approximately $350-400/month. Scaling: Snowflake warehouse can be resized to Small for faster processing if daily window is exceeded; MWAA can scale workers for parallel DAG tasks. Current X-Small warehouse processes a 200-unit fleet daily aggregation in under 15 minutes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v1 (completed): Daily idle aggregation with hardcoded OEM curves, global diesel price, weekly email reports. Phase 2 v2 (completed): Per-site diesel price via cost_centers, RPM profile models, report quality improvements. Phase 3 v3 (current): oem_fuel_curves dbt seed table replacing CASE expressions, fleet manager CSV update workflow, dbt singular test for curve coverage, documentation. Phase 4: Predictive maintenance alerts from RPM anomaly patterns, route optimization recommendations via Snowflake ML functions.
target_output.development_guidelines: oem_fuel_curves.csv must be the single source of truth for fuel consumption values — no hardcoded fuel constants in any dbt model SQL. dbt singular test asserting complete coverage of equipment_units by oem_fuel_curves must pass in CI before merge. New equipment models added via GitLab MR updating the seed CSV; fleet managers follow the documented template in docs/adding-equipment-models.md. dbt model SQL must use ref() for all cross-model references — no raw Snowflake table names in models.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: oem_fuel_curves seed values may be inaccurate for older equipment with degraded engines. Mitigation: fuel_consumption_logs from manual fill records can be used to validate and calibrate curve values; document this calibration process. Risk: fct_fuel_waste_daily LEFT JOIN against oem_fuel_curves will silently produce NULL waste estimates for equipment models not in the seed table. Mitigation: dbt singular test catches missing curves before merge; daily DAG logs warning for any NULL estimates. Tradeoff: dbt seed CSV over a Snowflake UI-managed table accepts that fleet managers need GitLab MR access to update curves, which is appropriate given the pipeline is code-first.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed dbt seed CSV over a managed Snowflake table for oem_fuel_curves; seed CSV is version-controlled, reviewable via GitLab MR diff, and deployable via existing CI pipeline. Confirmed LEFT JOIN with NULL warning over INNER JOIN that drops unmatched units; silent data loss from INNER JOIN would be worse than a visible NULL in the report that prompts curve addition.

metadata.notes: Round 3 pipeline revision migrating OEM fuel curves from CASE expressions to dbt seed reference table; Airflow/dbt/Snowflake construction equipment fuel analytics.
