previous_plan.title: Fleet Procurement TCO Analyzer - Desktop Architecture Plan
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Tauri 2.0 desktop application with a React 18 frontend and Rust computation backend enabling fleet procurement managers to build vehicle acquisition scenarios, compute multi-year TCO projections, and export PDF reports. Fully offline, SQLCipher-encrypted local storage, distributed as signed installers for macOS and Windows.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Tauri shell wraps a React SPA communicating with Rust command handlers via IPC. The Rust layer handles TCO calculations and SQLite persistence. No network connections permitted.
previous_plan.data_model: vehicle_scenarios, tco_models, depreciation_curves, fuel_cost_projections, lease_terms, procurement_decisions stored in SQLite.
previous_plan.workflows: Scenario creation and TCO computation described generically; depreciation method unspecified. PDF export mentioned without chart capture detail.
previous_plan.technology_stack: Tauri 2.0, React 18, TypeScript, Recharts, Tailwind CSS, Rust, rusqlite, SQLCipher, GitHub Actions.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision focuses on closing the two open issues from the auditor: ISS-001 (depreciation method scope) and ISS-002 (PDF chart fidelity). I am adding explicit support for straight-line and double-declining-balance depreciation with named Rust structs and parameters, and specifying the html2canvas + printpdf chart capture pipeline for PDF export. The Rust computation engine now names the rust_decimal crate for monetary precision. All other sections are strengthened to meet word count minimums while remaining specific to the fleet TCO domain.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined two supported depreciation methods (straight-line and double-declining-balance) with their input parameters in both the data_model entities and the workflows section. Added named Rust structs (StraightLineParams, DecliningBalanceParams) to the technology_stack and backend design.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Engineering can now scope and implement the TCO engine with clear method definitions; auditor ISS-001 should resolve.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified html2canvas snapshot strategy for capturing Recharts SVG output as PNG images, then embedding via the printpdf Rust crate. Added this detail to the workflows export section and frontend_risks mitigation.
fix_report.expected_outcome: PDF reports will contain faithful chart images; ISS-002 should resolve.

target_output.title: Fleet Procurement TCO Analyzer - Desktop Architecture Plan (Round 2)
target_output.executive_summary: A Tauri 2.0 desktop application for fleet procurement managers that computes multi-year total cost of ownership across vehicle acquisition scenarios. A React 18 frontend handles scenario configuration and Recharts visualization; a Rust command backend executes straight-line and double-declining-balance depreciation calculations, fuel cost projections with configurable escalation rates, and NPV analysis using the rust_decimal crate for monetary precision. All data is stored locally in a SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite database. Scenarios are exported as PDF reports with html2canvas-captured chart images embedded via the printpdf crate. Signed installers distributed for macOS 12+ (ARM and x86) and Windows 10+.
target_output.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 wraps a React 18 SPA in a native desktop shell on macOS and Windows. The React layer communicates exclusively via Tauri IPC commands to Rust handler modules: tco_engine (NPV, depreciation, fuel projection), scenario_commands (SQLite CRUD via rusqlite with SQLCipher), and pdf_commands (chart image capture coordination and PDF assembly via printpdf). No network connections are permitted; the Tauri allowlist explicitly blocks all HTTP and WebSocket access. The SQLCipher passphrase is derived from a machine-specific hardware UUID combined with a salt stored in the OS keychain (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager via the keyring Rust crate). GitHub Actions matrix builds produce signed .dmg installers (notarized via Apple Developer ID) and signed .msi packages (Authenticode) for distribution through the internal software portal.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Tauri 2.0 shell, React 18, TypeScript 5, React Router v6, Zustand 4 for scenario draft state, Recharts 2.x for TCO line/area/bar charts, Tailwind CSS, html2canvas 1.x for chart PNG capture. Backend: Rust 1.78, Tauri command handlers, rusqlite 0.31 with bundled SQLCipher feature, rust_decimal 1.35 for monetary arithmetic, printpdf 0.7 for PDF assembly, keyring 2.x for OS keychain access. Build: bun 1.3 for frontend deps, cargo for Rust, GitHub Actions matrix (macos-14 ARM, macos-13 x86, windows-latest x64), Apple Developer ID notarization, Authenticode signing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Scenario Builder: step-through form collecting vehicle class (light-duty, medium-duty, heavy-duty), acquisition method (lease or purchase), MSRP, residual value, money factor, financing rate, annual mileage, fuel type (diesel/gasoline/electric), fuel price with annual escalation rate, and analysis horizon (12-84 months). TCO Engine: computes per-month cost streams for depreciation (straight-line or double-declining-balance), fuel, estimated maintenance (configurable per-mile rate), and financing charges; sums to cumulative TCO. Comparison View: side-by-side area charts (cumulative TCO over time) and stacked bar charts (year-by-year cost component breakdown). PDF Export: generates a multi-page report with scenario assumption table, TCO summary table, and embedded Recharts chart PNG images. Scenario Library: persists all created scenarios in SQLite for recall and re-analysis.
target_output.system_components: Tauri 2.0 desktop shell (lightweight Wry webview, ~8 MB binary overhead). React SPA bundle (served from embedded asset directory, no local HTTP server). Rust command modules: tco_engine (StraightLineDepreciation, DecliningBalanceDepreciation, FuelProjection, NPVCalculator structs), scenario_commands (ScenarioRepository with rusqlite), pdf_commands (ChartCaptureBridge coordinating html2canvas invocation from JS, then passing PNG bytes to Rust printpdf builder). SQLite database file with SQLCipher encryption (AES-256-CBC) in user app data directory. Local JSON-lines log file for error and event capture. GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with multi-platform build matrix.
target_output.workflows: Scenario Creation and TCO Computation: Procurement manager opens the scenario builder, selects vehicle category and acquisition method (lease or purchase), enters MSRP ($85,000 for a Class 6 medium-duty truck), sets a 60-month analysis horizon, inputs diesel price at $4.10/gallon with 3% annual escalation, and chooses double-declining-balance depreciation with a 5-year useful life. On form submission, the React layer invokes the Tauri `compute_tco` command, passing a serialized ScenarioParams struct. The Rust tco_engine iterates months 1-60: computing monthly depreciation amounts via the DecliningBalanceDepreciation struct (book_value * 2/useful_life_months), projecting fuel cost using the FuelProjection struct with compounding escalation, and summing components into a TCO data series. Results are returned as a serialized TcoResult struct with monthly and cumulative arrays. Recharts renders the multi-series area chart in the comparison view. PDF Export Workflow: The manager clicks Export PDF; the React layer calls html2canvas on each Recharts chart container, captures PNG data URLs, then invokes the Tauri `generate_pdf` command with the PNG bytes and scenario data. The Rust pdf_commands module uses printpdf to assemble a multi-page PDF: cover page with scenario title and date, assumption table (vehicle specs, depreciation method, fuel inputs), TCO summary table, and one page per embedded chart PNG. The PDF is written to the user-selected file path via the Tauri file dialog.
target_output.data_model: vehicle_scenarios (id UUID, name TEXT, vehicle_class ENUM light_duty/medium_duty/heavy_duty, acquisition_method ENUM lease/purchase, msrp_cents INTEGER, analysis_horizon_months INTEGER, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME, parameters_json TEXT, results_json TEXT). tco_models (id UUID, scenario_id FK, depreciation_method ENUM straight_line/double_declining_balance, horizon_months INTEGER, discount_rate REAL, useful_life_years INTEGER, salvage_value_cents INTEGER, declining_rate REAL NULLABLE, created_at DATETIME). depreciation_curves (id UUID, scenario_id FK, month INTEGER, book_value_cents INTEGER, depreciation_amount_cents INTEGER). fuel_cost_projections (id UUID, scenario_id FK, year INTEGER, price_per_gallon_cents INTEGER, annual_fuel_cost_cents INTEGER, escalation_rate REAL). lease_terms (id UUID, scenario_id FK, monthly_payment_cents INTEGER, term_months INTEGER, residual_value_cents INTEGER, money_factor REAL, acquisition_fee_cents INTEGER). procurement_decisions (id UUID, scenario_id FK, recommendation ENUM lease/purchase, rationale TEXT, decided_at DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: Tauri IPC command surface (no HTTP): `compute_tco(params: ScenarioParams) -> Result<TcoResult, TcoError>` — runs TCO engine and returns monthly cost arrays; `save_scenario(scenario: ScenarioInput) -> Result<Uuid, DbError>` — persists scenario and computed results to SQLite; `list_scenarios() -> Result<Vec<ScenarioSummary>, DbError>` — returns all saved scenarios with name, horizon, and created_at; `load_scenario(id: Uuid) -> Result<ScenarioDetail, DbError>` — loads full scenario with results for chart rendering; `delete_scenario(id: Uuid) -> Result<(), DbError>`; `generate_pdf(request: PdfRequest) -> Result<(), PdfError>` — assembles PDF from chart PNGs and scenario data and writes to the specified file path; `get_log_path() -> String` — returns local log file path for support diagnostics.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256-CBC encryption on the local database file; passphrase derived from a machine-specific hardware UUID and a per-installation salt stored in the OS keychain via the Rust keyring crate (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager). Tauri allowlist configured with no network permissions: `http: { all: false }`, `shell: { open: false }`. App binary signed with Apple Developer ID and notarized via notarytool for macOS Gatekeeper approval; Authenticode-signed .msi for Windows SmartScreen clearance. Local log file records scenario export events and computation errors but contains no cost values or vehicle data. SQLite integrity_check invoked on app startup to detect database file corruption before user operations.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions matrix workflow with three parallel jobs: `build-macos-arm` (macos-14 runner, target aarch64-apple-darwin), `build-macos-x86` (macos-13 runner, target x86_64-apple-darwin), `build-windows` (windows-latest runner, target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc). Each job: checkout repo, install Rust toolchain via rustup, install bun and run `bun install`, run `bun run tauri build --target <target>`. macOS jobs submit to Apple notarytool using APPLE_DEVELOPER_ID and APPLE_NOTARIZATION_PASSWORD GitHub secrets. Windows job signs .msi with Authenticode cert stored as a GitHub secret. On tag push (v*.*.*): all three targets build, artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases. Installer size validated in CI: jobs fail if .dmg or .msi exceeds 50 MB. Distribution via internal software portal (Jira Service Desk attachment or shared SharePoint folder). Rollback: previous installer version retained on GitHub Releases; users can downgrade by re-running prior installer.
target_output.observability: Local JSON-lines log file at {app_data}/logs/fleet-tco.log capturing: `scenario_created` (scenario_id, name, acquisition_method, horizon_months), `tco_computed` (scenario_id, duration_ms, depreciation_method), `pdf_exported` (scenario_id, page_count, file_size_bytes), `db_integrity_check` (result: ok/corrupt), `rust_error` (command, error_type, message). Log rotation at 10 MB (rename to fleet-tco.log.1, start fresh). No remote telemetry or APM. Support workflow: user shares log file via IT help desk ticket. Key diagnostic metrics derivable from logs: computation duration per scenario, export frequency, error rates per command.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost: fully local application with no cloud services. Development cost drivers: multi-platform CI build time (~25 min per full matrix run). Apple Developer Program membership ($99/year) for notarization. Windows EV Authenticode certificate (~$400/year) for SmartScreen trust. Distribution via existing internal software portal at no incremental cost. Scaling: each additional platform target (Linux) adds one GitHub Actions job. No per-user cost beyond initial software distribution.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Tauri 2.0 project scaffold with Rust TCO engine supporting straight-line and double-declining-balance depreciation, fuel cost projection with escalation, lease payment stream calculation, and NPV. React scenario builder form with Zustand state. Recharts comparison charts (cumulative TCO area, cost breakdown bar). SQLite persistence with SQLCipher. PDF export via html2canvas + printpdf. macOS ARM signed installer. Phase 2 (3 weeks): Windows x64 and macOS x86 build targets. SQLite integrity_check on startup. Scenario library list view with search and sort. Local log rotation. Internal software portal distribution. Phase 3 (post-pilot, 4 weeks): Multiple depreciation method comparison in a single scenario (side-by-side straight-line vs. double-declining). Fleet-wide batch scenario import via CSV. Configurable maintenance cost curve (flat per-mile vs. age-adjusted). User-defined depreciation presets saved to SQLite.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust commands must return typed Result enums (TcoError, DbError, PdfError) — no panic paths in command handlers. All monetary values stored and computed as integers (cents) using rust_decimal; conversion to display strings only at the React boundary. TypeScript strict mode; no `any` types in scenario parameter or result interfaces. Component tests for the scenario builder form using React Testing Library. Rust unit tests for TCO engine functions (straight-line depreciation, declining balance, fuel escalation, NPV) using cargo test with known fixture inputs and expected cent-precision outputs. PR requires one approval from a team member who has reviewed both the Rust computation logic and the React chart integration. Semantic versioning: MAJOR for breaking schema changes requiring database migration, MINOR for new features, PATCH for bug fixes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SQLite database migration complexity when adding new scenario parameters in future versions. Mitigation: version the parameters_json column schema and run migrations on app startup using a simple version table. Risk: html2canvas chart capture fails for complex Recharts SVG patterns (gradients, custom tooltips). Mitigation: strip non-essential SVG features from chart configs used in export mode; test all chart types against html2canvas in CI. Tradeoff: chose Tauri over Electron for the 50 MB installer size constraint; tradeoff is that Tauri's webview is the OS-native one (WKWebView on macOS, WebView2 on Windows), introducing minor rendering differences that must be tested on both platforms. Risk: Apple notarization API latency causing CI build times to exceed 30 minutes on release builds. Mitigation: run notarization only on tag-triggered workflows, not on PR builds. Tradeoff: double-declining-balance depreciation is supported but MACRS is deferred to Phase 3, as MACRS requires tax year tables that increase engine complexity disproportionately for the MVP scope.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Depreciation methods: straight-line (useful life, salvage value) and double-declining-balance (useful life, declining rate) are supported in MVP; MACRS deferred to Phase 3 due to tax table complexity. PDF chart capture: html2canvas PNG snapshot strategy selected over headless Chromium (too heavy for an offline desktop tool) and SVG serialization (lossy for complex chart fills). Monetary precision: rust_decimal crate confirmed for all cost calculations; floating-point arithmetic explicitly prohibited in the TCO engine.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolves ISS-001 (depreciation method scope) and ISS-002 (PDF chart fidelity) for a Tauri/Rust fleet TCO desktop tool. Most sections strong; cost_and_scaling and development_guidelines are at moderate depth appropriate for a local desktop tool.
